questions.stem: Which idea is talked about in the first paragraph of the reading?

options.A: How you should do Tabata training.
options.B: What is the best time for Tabata training.
options.C: Who first had the idea of Tabata training.
options.D: How often you should do Tabata training.

questions.passage:
Tabata training is a very popular way of exercising these days. It doesn't take much time or space, and it burns calories faster than other ways of exercising. The idea of Tabata training is simple: exercise for 20 seconds, rest for 10 seconds, and then repeat (at least eight times). The moves for the 20-second exercise are not difficult to learn. Jumping jacks, high knees, squats, and planks are some of the most common moves. You can decide yourself what moves to do in your Tabata training. For example, you can do more leg exercises if you want strong legs.

One great thing about Tabata training is that your body will keep burning calories for at least an hour after 4 minutes of Tabata training. But to have this wonderful "afterburn," you need to exercise really hard during each 20 seconds. If you seldom exercise or have heart problems, this exercise may not be good for you. But for people who enjoy exercising but are too busy to go to the gym, Tabata training might just be the answer.
questions.explain: 第一段說明 Tabata 的做法（運動 20 秒、休息 10 秒、重複、動作種類），即「該怎麼做」，選 A。

questions.stem: Who might find that Tabata training is right for them?

options.B: People who want to start exercising.
options.C: People who want to fix their heart problems.
options.D: People who already have a habit of exercising.

questions.passage:
Tabata training is a very popular way of exercising these days. It doesn't take much time or space, and it burns calories faster than other ways of exercising. The idea of Tabata training is simple: exercise for 20 seconds, rest for 10 seconds, and then repeat (at least eight times). The moves for the 20-second exercise are not difficult to learn. Jumping jacks, high knees, squats, and planks are some of the most common moves. You can decide yourself what moves to do in your Tabata training. For example, you can do more leg exercises if you want strong legs.

One great thing about Tabata training is that your body will keep burning calories for at least an hour after 4 minutes of Tabata training. But to have this wonderful "afterburn," you need to exercise really hard during each 20 seconds. If you seldom exercise or have heart problems, this exercise may not be good for you. But for people who enjoy exercising but are too busy to go to the gym, Tabata training might just be the answer.
questions.explain: 文中說少運動或有心臟問題者不適合，適合喜歡運動但太忙的人，即已有運動習慣者，選 D。

questions.stem:
There are four important points in the report:
a. What "No Overtime Day" is
b. Why "No Overtime Day" fails
c. Why there is "No Overtime Day" in the country
d. How workers deal with "No Overtime Day"
How are they ordered in the report?
questions.passage:
The problem of long working hours is a real headache in our country. It kills many people every year (see Figure 1), and many companies are trying to change that. They have at least one "No Overtime Day" in a week, and it is often Wednesday. On "No Overtime Day," workers are asked to leave their offices before 8 p.m. At 8 p.m., companies play the song "There's Always Tomorrow" and turn off all the lights.
     However, after the workers walk out of their offices, they don't go home. Some go back later, turn on their table lamps, and keep working, and others find somewhere else to work until very late at night. Restaurants and coffee shops are busier on Wednesday evenings, and they have started selling drinks and meals for these workers.
     But why do these people keep working? A study shows that workers who work more hours often have a bigger chance to get paid more and rise higher in their companies. This is true for both men and women (see Figure 2). Working long hours has become a way to show that people are hard-working. Clearly, this must be changed before the problem gets any worse.
questions.explain: 文章先講為何設無加班日(c)、無加班日是什麼(a)、員工如何應對(d)、最後說明為何失敗(b)，順序為 cadb，選 D。

questions.stem: What does this mean in the report?

options.A: The way workers show they are hard-working.
options.B: Restaurants and coffee shops open until very late at night.
options.C: Workers do not go home when they walk out of the office.
options.D: The number of workers who get paid more and rise higher in the company.

questions.stem: What does resentful mean in the reading?
questions.passage:
Language and Power: Cameroon's Story
By Elisa Grant, 2 Oct 2017

In the modern world, being able to speak English is often a way to get power. However, this is not true for English speakers in Cameroon. To know why things are different in this African country, we need to go back one hundred years.

In 1919, Kamerun (the name of Cameroon at the time) was cut into two parts: one belonged to France, and the other belonged to the UK. In 1960, the French Cameroon became the Republic of Cameroun, and the UK's part joined it in 1961. The new country changed its name to the Federal Republic of Cameroon. Although both French and English are Cameroon's official languages, only around 20% of its people speak English. Also, the government has been in French speakers' hands since the two parts became one country.

For a long time, Cameroon's English speakers have felt they are not welcome in their own country. For example, it is very difficult for them to find jobs in the government, and they are often asked to speak French in business and at official events. They have become more resentful at the government and decided to fight for themselves. Since last year, the English speakers have tried to build their own country. Yesterday, they said in a public meeting that they were not part of Cameroon anymore. They are now a new country, "Ambazonia." When the police tried to stop the meeting, at least eight people were killed.
questions.explain: 英語族群長期不受歡迎、決定起而反抗，resentful 指對政府的「憤怒、不滿」，最接近 Angry，選 B。

questions.stem: What does Cameroon's government most likely think of Ambazonia?

options.A: It plans to do business with Ambazonia.
options.B: It needs Ambazonia for money and help.
options.C: It does not want to be part of Ambazonia.
options.D: It does not agree that Ambazonia is a country.

options.B: Why English speakers in Cameroon have less power.

options.D: Why only 20% of the people in Cameroon speak English.

questions.stem: What is recommended to people who are visiting the Southend Trail?

options.C: Hiking one part of the trail a day.
options.D: Visiting the museum in the morning.

questions.passage:
THE SOUTHEND TRAIL has been popular with nature lovers for years. The 120-km trail passes beautiful lakes, crosses rivers, and goes through mountains and hills. This gives bird lovers some of the best places for birdwatching. The trail also takes you to the Southend Museum and to two famous castles, Edward Castle and Sloan Castle.
It's best to plan one day for one part of the trail and start your hike early in the morning, because each part takes at least seven hours. If you don't want to walk, you can bike, but make sure you stay on the main trail, because the side trails are not wide enough for biking.
There are two types of lodging for visitors. Camping is popular in summer, but it is only allowed on a few campgrounds (see the map). A more comfortable one is to stay at a hotel in one of the towns. You can find some very nice ones that also have breakfast.
questions.explain: 文中建議一天走一段步道並清晨出發，因每段至少要走七小時，選 C。

questions.stem: A palindrome is a word or a sentence that reads the same from left to right or from right to left, ______, "eye," "Bob," "my gym," and "Was it a car or a cat I saw?"
questions.passage:
English words are made of 26 letters, and palindromes and anagrams are two kinds of word games about spelling. A palindrome is a word or a sentence that reads the same from left to right or from right to left, __40__, "eye," "Bob," "my gym," and "Was it a car or a cat I saw?" An anagram of a word or words is made by putting the letters of the word or words in a different way. Look at the words and their anagrams below. Can you think of other possible anagrams of these words?

Anagrams are often longer words that don't really mean anything but are fun to say. Sometimes they can even mean something __42__, like when a common word, "restaurant," becomes "Eat rats, run!" Actually, palindromes and anagrams are __43__. Palindromes can be used to learn mathematics and make music. Anagrams are also a good way to hide something. In history, people often hid their important studies in anagrams. Can you think of any other way to use them?
questions.explain: 空格後列舉 eye、Bob 等例子，用 for example 引出舉例，選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Lynn from the dialogue?

options.B: She likes to try new food.
options.C: She did not enjoy the food at Caldron.
options.D: She changed Joey's idea about Caldron.

questions.passage:
Joey: Lynn, have you tried Caldron? I've heard that restaurant is good.
Lynn: I have. I went there once for Tom's party.
Joey: And?
Lynn: Well, I never thought I would have to cook for myself when eating out.
Joey: But it's fun to cook your own food.
Lynn: I don't see any fun in that. Besides, I want nice food.
Joey: So you didn't like the food there?
Lynn: Everything tasted the same. I mean, they were all cooked in the same boiling pot. How could they taste different? I just kept throwing strange things into the pot. I didn't even know what half the things were made of.
Joey: Don't worry. Everything should be safe to eat after a hot bath in the pot. And you didn't get sick after that, right? Besides, you tried something different there. Still a good experience, I think?
Lynn: I don't think we share the same idea of good experiences.
questions.explain: Lynn 抱怨食物味道都一樣、要自己煮，明顯不喜歡 Caldron 的餐點，故選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know about Woollie from Katie's diary?

options.B: Woollie was taken to visit sick kids.
options.C: Woollie was sick when he ran away.
options.D: Woollie looked strange without his fleece.

questions.passage:
Dear Diary,                    June 7, 2011
    I was very sad to hear what happened to Woollie yesterday. Woollie was my favorite sheep. I have a storybook about Woollie. It says when Woollie was four, he ran away from Mr. Armstrong's farm, and it took Mr. Armstrong six years to find Woollie. But Woollie had grown so much fleece that he didn't even look like a sheep. Mr. Armstrong decided to cut his fleece on TV so everyone could see. Daddy said this happened on the day I was born. Woollie's fleece weighed 27 kg, and could make clothes for 20 large men. I asked Daddy if his favorite vest was made from Woollie's fleece. Daddy said no, because Woollie's fleece was sold to collect money for sick kids. In the storybook, Woollie was taken to see Ms. Stella Clark, the leader of our city then. They even had tea together. Daddy said Woollie did meet Ms. Clark, but he wasn't sure if they had tea. Daddy said Woollie was put down because he was too sick and there was no way to help him. I cried. I hope Woollie will be happy up there in the sky.
questions.explain: Woollie 剃毛上電視是 Katie 出生那天，而剃毛前 Woollie 已四歲又走失六年，故 Woollie 年紀比 Katie 大，選 A。

I asked Daddy if his favorite vest was made from Woollie's fleece. Daddy said no, because Woollie's fleece was sold to collect money for sick kids. In the storybook, Woollie was taken to see Ms. Stella Clark, the leader of our city then. They even had tea together. Daddy said Woollie did meet Ms. Clark, but he wasn't sure if they had tea. Daddy said Woollie was put down because he was too sick and there was no way to help him. I cried. I hope Woollie will be happy up there in the sky.

questions.stem: What can we learn about The Piano Lesson from the reading?

options.A: Matisse painted it when his son was away from home.
options.B: The woman who sits on a seat behind the boy is his mother.
options.C: Matisse uses yellow and red to show that his son was happy.
options.D: The candle on the piano shows that the piano lesson has just begun.

questions.passage:
Here's The Piano Lesson, one of Henri Matisse's most famous paintings. The boy in the picture is the painter's son, Pierre. In the picture, Pierre looks quite serious. Why? Well, look at the yellow candle on the red piano. It's almost burned down. So maybe Pierre has already practiced for hours. And Matisse uses a lot of gray, not a very fun color, right? It shows how the poor kid feels, don't you think?
    On the top right corner, you can see a woman sitting straight on a seat. Is she Pierre's piano teacher? Or his mother? Well, if you're a Matisse fan, you'll know this is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool. Interesting, right? Matisse likes to do that a lot. In fact, there's another work by Matisse in the picture. Did you find it? Right! The sculpture on the bottom left corner!
    Matisse painted this picture in 1916. Pierre was already 16 then, but the artist painted his son much younger than he really was. Pierre had gone away to be a soldier, and Matisse didn't know whether he would come back. Maybe this is a worried father's way to remember the good old days.
questions.explain: 文末提到 Pierre 已從軍離家，Matisse 在他不在家時作畫，故選 A。

options.A: Put his family in his paintings.
options.B: Paint to remember the good old days.
options.C: Use a lot of fun colors in his art works.
options.D: Put his earlier art work in a new painting.

It shows how the poor kid feels, don't you think?
    On the top right corner, you can see a woman sitting straight on a seat. Is she Pierre's piano teacher? Or his mother? Well, if you're a Matisse fan, you'll know this is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool. Interesting, right? Matisse likes to do that a lot. In fact, there's another work by Matisse in the picture. Did you find it? Right! The sculpture on the bottom left corner!
    Matisse painted this picture in 1916. Pierre was already 16 then, but the artist painted his son much younger than he really was. Pierre had gone away to be a soldier, and Matisse didn't know whether he would come back. Maybe this is a worried father's way to remember the good old days.
questions.explain: do that 緊接在把舊畫 Woman on a High Stool 放進新畫之後，指的是把自己先前的作品放進新畫中，選 D。

questions.stem: What is NOT used in the reading to refer to Henri Matisse?
questions.passage:
Here's The Piano Lesson, one of Henri Matisse's most famous paintings. The boy in the picture is the painter's son, Pierre. In the picture, Pierre looks quite serious. Why? Well, look at the yellow candle on the red piano. It's almost burned down. So maybe Pierre has already practiced for hours. And Matisse uses a lot of gray, not a very fun color, right? It shows how the poor kid feels, don't you think?
    On the top right corner, you can see a woman sitting straight on a seat. Is she Pierre's piano teacher? Or his mother? Well, if you're a Matisse fan, you'll know this is in fact another painting by him, Woman on a High Stool. Interesting, right? Matisse likes to do that a lot. In fact, there's another work by Matisse in the picture. Did you find it? Right! The sculpture on the bottom left corner!
    Matisse painted this picture in 1916. Pierre was already 16 then, but the artist painted his son much younger than he really was. Pierre had gone away to be a soldier, and Matisse didn't know whether he would come back. Maybe this is a worried father's way to remember the good old days.
questions.explain: the poor kid 指的是畫中的兒子 Pierre，不是 Matisse；artist、painter、worried father 才是指 Matisse，故選 C。

questions.stem: What idea is talked about in the reading?

options.A: How Teachers' Day is celebrated now.
options.B: Why Teachers' Day was celebrated at first.
options.C: Why Confucius was seen as a great teacher.
options.D: How teachers today feel about Teachers' Day.

questions.passage:
On Teachers' Day, we thank our teachers for their hard work. We also celebrate this day to remember Confucius, the great Chinese teacher from 2,500 years ago. Well, that's everything we're taught about Teachers' Day. But, the first Teachers' Day was not on Confucius's birthday, and it was not about thanking our teachers.
    In 1930, Mr. Tai Shuang-qiu and other teachers celebrated the first Teachers' Day in Nanjing. But there was nothing to celebrate. Teachers were paid very little and not respected. These teachers took this chance to shout out their problems. They had Teachers' Day on June 6 because the date was easy to remember and near the end of the school year. This action by Mr. Tai and the other teachers was welcomed and followed by teachers from other cities. In 1939, the government made Teachers' Day a national holiday. However, it was on August 27, the day when Confucius's birthday was celebrated. Not everyone hailed this decision: Teachers' Day was never about Confucius, and Confucius couldn't speak for all the teachers either.
    One funny thing was that the government was wrong about the date of Confucius's birthday. In 1952, people found out he was in fact born on September 28. That was when we started to celebrate Teachers' Day on Confucius's real birthday.
questions.explain: 全文重點在說明教師節最初為何設立（教師爭取權益），故選 B。

questions.stem: Which is true about Teachers' Day from the reading?

options.A: It used to be celebrated on different dates.
options.B: It was started by students for their teachers.
options.C: It has been celebrated in Nanjing for over 2,500 years.
options.D: Teachers decided to celebrate it on Confucius's birthday.

options.A: Not everyone cared about the decision.
options.C: Not everyone heard about the decision.

questions.stem: What does the writer try to do with this reading?

options.A: To talk about a problem that no one notices.
options.B: To share a fun experience that was not common.
options.C: To tell a piece of history that few people know about.
options.D: To give an example of an idea that is hard to understand.

questions.stem: Why does National Formosa Railway write this letter?

options.A: There is a train ticket sale.
options.B: A train line will be closed in July.
options.C: It will open a new train line in August.
options.D: Some of its trains will run on different lines.

questions.passage:
Dear Traveler,
    You have bought an NFR ticket online for a train that runs between White Water City and Cloud City.
    We are sorry to tell you that, because of the typhoon last month, the Sand Town – Spring Town line will be closed between August 14 and 28 2016. During this time, some trains will change lines and will not stop at a few stations. There will be free buses to take travelers to these stations. Please check below for the changes. Visit http://www.nfr.gov/ for more information.
    We wish you a nice summer.

Lena Muller
National Formosa Railway
questions.explain: 信中說明因颱風封閉路線，部分列車將改走不同路線，故選 D。

questions.stem: Below are the ideas that are talked about in the reading. a. The problems Darayya has. b. How Darayya's library was started. c. What makes Darayya's library special. d. How Darayya's library helps people there. In what order does the writer put his ideas in the reading?
questions.passage:
In Darayya, a city in Syria, there's a library, and it has 15,000 books on almost every subject you can think of. However, it is different from any libraries you know: It is a secret underground library, and only people in Darayya know where it is.
    Over the years, war has shaken Darayya badly. Every day, houses are bombed and people are killed. Stores are closed one after another, and so are schools. To help the kids in Darayya with their learning, Anas Ahmad, a 19-year-old student, and his friends decided to build a library. They built the library under the ground to keep it safe from bombing. But it is dangerous to collect books for the library. Often, Ahmad and his friends look for books in houses that were bombed. They need to be careful because they may be killed in another bombing.
    You may ask, "In a place like Darayya, would people be interested in books?" "Just like the body needs food, the mind needs books," says one library user. In the library, people enjoy their time of reading and forget about the terrible world above, so their life doesn't seem so hard. Through reading, they are able to dream of a better life after war.
questions.explain: 第一段講圖書館的特別（c）、第二段講戰爭問題（a）與圖書館如何建立（b）、第三段講如何幫助人們（d），順序為 c a b d，選 D。

questions.stem: Why do people in Darayya go to the library even during the war?

options.B: They find joy and hope in reading.
options.C: They want to learn how to win the war.
options.D: Their school teachers give lessons there.

options.A: It was built during the war.
options.B: It has been bombed many times.
options.C: It was built to remember people who died in the war.
options.D: Most of its books are collected from outside of Darayya.

questions.stem: We know we told you that the party would be in ______. However, we just moved to Carlton City last month, so the party won't be in Pattersons Town.
questions.passage:
Hello Dave,

We're happy to invite you and your family to Daniel's fifth birthday party this Saturday.

We know we told you that the party would be in   35  . However, we just moved to Carlton City last month, so the party won't be in Pattersons Town. It'll be at Mosman Garden right next to Victor Zoo, just across from our new apartment. Please don't go to Northbank Park next to our old house.

The weather report said that it may be rainy this weekend, but   36  . If it rains, we'll move the party inside our home. However, if we're   37   and it is fine, we'll still have the party at the garden.

Again, we're all very happy and excited to welcome you to join us for some fun this weekend!

Best,
Mia, Sam & Daniel
questions.explain: 下文說別去舊家旁的 Northbank Park，可知原本告知的地點就是 Northbank Park，故選 D。

questions.stem: The weather report said that it may be rainy this weekend, but ______. If it rains, we'll move the party inside our home.

options.A: we have a plan if this happens
options.B: we could just wait for the rain to stop
options.C: we should be fine if the rain isn't too heavy
options.D: we will let you know if we'll still have the party

questions.stem: The stories I'd heard about the park ______ into my head at this moment. Anyone with a clear mind would just turn back.
questions.passage:
Usually I wouldn't cross the park at this time of night. But walking around the park would take more time, and it was raining so hard that I couldn't even see clearly what was right before me. So I entered the park. And that was the first stupid thing I did tonight.
    Soon after I walked into the park, I saw a man under a tree up ahead. My heart fell. The stories I'd heard about the park   38   into my head at this moment. Anyone with a clear mind would just turn back. But me? No. I did   39   stupid thing: I decided to hurry past him.
    Just when I was passing the man, he raised his head and gave me the strangest smile ever. I got scared and started running. "Hey!" the man shouted from behind. "Wait!" He was running after me!
    I ran like crazy. I had hoped the trees would keep some rain off. And they   40  . But they also made the park look even darker. I couldn't see what was ahead of me. Then, I ran into something. It was the man!
    "Don't kill me!" I cried.
    "What? I   41   to give back your bag! You dropped it," the man gave me my bag.
    Now, I felt saying that to the man was the stupidest thing I did tonight.
questions.explain: 全文以過去式敘述，此刻各種故事一一湧上心頭，用過去進行式 were all coming，選 C。

questions.stem: But me? No. I did ______ stupid thing: I decided to hurry past him.

questions.stem: My mom puts the ______ on to keep her hands away from water when she washes the dishes.
questions.explain: 洗碗時為了不讓手碰到水，會戴上手套（gloves）。手套是戴在手上的，符合語意。

questions.stem: Can you ______ your feet a little off the floor? I want to see if my keys are under the sofa.
questions.explain: 要看沙發底下有沒有鑰匙，會請對方把腳「抬起」離開地板，raise 為抬高之意。

questions.stem: ______ of my sisters are older than I am. I'm the youngest of the three children in my family.
questions.explain: 家中三個小孩，說話者最小，代表兩個姊姊都比自己大，用 Both（兩者都）。

questions.stem: The beach near our town is a very ______ place. Every summer at least one kid dies there in the water.
questions.explain: 每年夏天至少有一名孩子在水裡喪命，可見這片海灘很危險（dangerous）。

questions.stem: My wife enjoys going to the movies with her friends, but I like to watch TV at home by ______.
questions.explain: 片語 by oneself 表示「獨自」，主詞為 I，故用反身代名詞 myself。

questions.stem: For the past twenty years, my father ______ in a school library. But he'll leave the job next month.
questions.explain: For the past twenty years 表示過去持續到現在的動作，需用現在完成式 has worked。

questions.stem: It's great that we can buy train tickets over the phone without going out. It ______ us a lot of time.
questions.explain: 不用出門就能買票，因此替我們「省下」很多時間，save time 為省時間。

questions.stem: Mozart ______ his first music when he was only six years old.
questions.explain: when he was only six 為過去明確時間點，用過去簡單式 wrote。

questions.stem:
Jill: Have you decided ______ you will celebrate your 30th birthday?
Sue: Yeah, I'm going to have a big barbecue party.
questions.explain: 回答提到要辦烤肉派對，屬於「用什麼方式」慶生，故用 how。

questions.stem: Alison doesn't like ______ what to do. She only does things she wants to do.
questions.explain: Alison 是「被告知」該做什麼，need 被動不定詞 to be told；like 後接不定詞。

questions.stem: Josh has planned to make a trip to New York and ______ some of his friends there.
questions.explain: and 連接 to make a trip 的 make 與 visit，兩個原形動詞並列，故用 visit。

questions.stem: Ms. Johnson has been taking phone calls since she entered the office this morning. Just when she thought she could finally leave work, ______ call came in.
questions.explain: 她一直接電話，正想下班時「又」有一通電話進來，another 表「又一（不特定）」。

questions.stem: We were so sure that Jerry ______ well on the difficult job. His past experience in other work showed he was the right guy for it. So when he failed, no one believed it.
questions.explain: 過去時間點對未來的預期，用過去未來式 would do（原以為他會做得好）。

questions.stem: For Mike, the price is ______ important thing when he shops for jeans. He cares even more about the shape and the size of the pockets.
questions.explain: 他更在意版型與口袋，代表價格是「最不」重要的，用最高級 the least important。

questions.stem: What is it in the reading?
questions.passage:
When it arrives,
Everyone cheers.
The shirts and shorts dance
On the clothesline,
The trees and flowers wave
Like they're saying hi,
And the clouds are so excited
To have a running race.

When it comes,
Dad's face falls.
His coat and jeans jump
Off the clothesline
Into the pond.
The tree leaves hop off
And have a party on his new car.

When it gets here,
A lot of fun things I notice:
The pond's face,
Sad and old with lines;
Mrs. Smith's bread,
Delicious and freshly baked.
But what smell is it?
Oh, no, it's from Mr. Brown's feet!

(leaf (leaves) 葉子)
questions.explain: 衣物在曬衣繩上舞動、雲朵賽跑、樹葉飛落、把味道吹來，都是風（wind）造成的現象。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Buffy's friends?

options.A: Matt has experience of yard sales.
options.B: Jamie had a successful yard sale last week.
options.C: Albert thinks weather is important for yard sales.
options.D: Debby thinks things are cheaper online than at yard sales.

questions.passage:
Buffy: Hey, guys! I have a lot of things I don't need, so I'm thinking about having a yard sale. What do you think?

Debby: Having a yard sale is not easy. You have to collect things, put prices on them, put them out on tables and then wait for people to come. And usually you have to sell things at VERY low prices. For all that time and work, you make very little money. Why not just sell your things online for higher prices?

Jamie: Debby's right. But I think it'd be even better to just give your things to people who need them. I just sent out a lot of things last week.

Albert: Hey, I love yard sales! It's a great chance to make big money if you pick the right day. In my experience, Saturday is the best.

Matt: I agree with Albert. You can make money and meet interesting people. I made some good friends at my last yard sale.

（原題為對話框圖示，發話者姓名依對話內容與官方答案還原標註）
questions.explain: Matt 說 I made some good friends at my last yard sale，可知他有辦跳蚤市場的經驗，選 A。

questions.stem: What do Buffy's friends think about having a yard sale?

options.A: Albert and Matt think it's a nice way to make friends.
options.B: Debby and Albert think it's difficult to make money from it.
options.C: Matt agrees with Jamie that it's a good chance to help people.
options.D: Jamie agrees with Debby that it's a lot of trouble to prepare for it.

questions.passage:

From Jan. 4 to Feb. 4, for each $50 you spend (on anything EXCEPT books and CDs) at Sunny Market, you'll get a Sunny Sun picture. (2 pictures for every $100, 3 pictures for every $150, …)

questions.stem: What does them mean in the reading?
questions.passage:
Now I'm going to show you how to work with chocolate. I'll do it in a “bain-marie,” or, well, some people call it “water bath.” I myself love the name “bain-marie.” To make a “bain-marie,” you need two pots, one bigger than the other.
First, break the chocolate into small pieces and put them in the smaller pot. Usually when making chocolate desserts, you'll need to mix chocolate with butter and sugar. So if you do, put them in the pot too.
Now, half fill the bigger pot with warm water. Put the smaller pot over the bigger one, and start to slowly mix the chocolate, the butter, and the sugar together. Keep the water under 50ºC, or the chocolate may lose its shine. And make sure that no water goes in the smaller pot, or the chocolate will become hard and cannot be used.
Keep mixing for about five to ten minutes, and the job is done. Easy, right?

(mix 混合)
questions.explain: put them in the pot too 中的 them 指前一句提到要一起混合的 butter and sugar，選 B。

options.B: The water in the pot should not be warmer than 50ºC.
options.C: It is better to finish mixing it in less than five minutes.
options.D: We should not mix butter and sugar together at the same time.

questions.stem: What does the news story say about the Yangs' food truck?

options.A: What people love about their food.
options.B: What made them start their business.
options.C: How they made delicious fried chicken.
options.D: How they fixed their business problems.

questions.passage:
Taiwanese Bian-dang in Manhattan, New York City
5/30/2014    by Eva Schmidt

During lunch time, on 53rd Street, you'll see a long line of people in front of a Taiwanese food truck for their “bian-dang,” a Taiwanese word for “lunch box.” On the menu you can find fried chicken with rice, pork dumplings, tea eggs, and other popular Taiwanese dishes.
The owners of the food truck are Thomas and Diana Yang, two Taiwanese-Americans. This brother-sister team started their business in 2009. Back then, there were a few Taiwanese restaurants in the city and several thousand food trucks, but the Yangs were the first to sell Taiwanese food on a food truck.
At first, business was slack. People thought they were just another Chinese food truck, and the food truck's name, “Cravings,” often made people think of desserts, not lunch. Few people would stop by and try their food. “I couldn't even give away free food,” said Thomas. To catch people's eye, they painted their truck blue, and drew Taiwan's national flower, the plum blossom, on it. They also changed the food truck's name to “bian-dang.” Soon people started to notice them, and business finally got better. Now on a good day, they can sell about 150 bian-dangs in about two hours.
So what's next for Thomas and Diana? Well, they hope one day there will be a block with Taiwanese food in the city, just like there is a Chinatown, a Korea Town, and a Little Italy. And their bian-dang food truck might just help to make that happen.

(national 國家的)
questions.explain: 報導描述生意起初冷清、被誤認、後來如何把餐車漆藍、改名解決問題，重點在他們如何解決生意困境，選 D。

questions.stem: What problem is Selena Bieber trying to fix?

options.A: High school lessons are too difficult.
options.B: Classes start too early in the morning.
options.C: Too many students are late for school.
options.D: Lunch break is too short for taking a rest.

questions.passage:
This is a letter by Selena Bieber to the students in her school.
http://www.nvhsstudsup.org/openletter

Later Is Better!
Selena Bieber
Northville High School

Do you feel it's hard to get up early for school and even harder to listen well in the first class at 8 a.m.? Well, this happens to most of us and is not helpful for our learning!

Some studies show that teenagers' brains make melatonin, the hormone that helps sleep, between 11 p.m. and 8 a.m. So it's better for us to sleep during these nine hours. But here comes the problem. We're asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m. That means we have to get up before 7, when our brains should still be sleeping. That's why we're always so tired and can't think clearly in early morning classes. No clear head, no good learning, right?

If we can't change how our body works, why can't school start later? In this way, we can not only sleep more but also learn better!

Want to help us make it happen?
Visit www.nvhsstudsup.org/later-better to learn more.

(brain 大腦　hormone 荷爾蒙)
questions.explain: 整封信主張上課時間太早，害學生睡不夠、早上第一節無法專心，訴求學校晚點上課，選 B。

options.A: Too much melatonin can hurt teenagers' brain.
options.B: The brain stops making melatonin after 11 p.m.
options.C: Sleeping longer helps the body make more melatonin.
options.D: It is easier to fall asleep when the brain is making melatonin.

questions.stem:
Below is some information from another study.
“We studied 48 high school students and found that the students who went to bed earlier did better on their school tests. This is not only true for those who slept nine hours, but also true for those who slept less. And some of them agreed that they learned better in early morning classes.”

Do the ideas in Selena Bieber's letter agree with the information?

options.A: Yes, because her letter says it is better to sleep from 11 p.m. to 8 a.m.
options.B: Yes, because her letter says getting up early is not helpful for learning.
options.C: No, because her letter says students are asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m.
options.D: No, because her letter says teenagers' brains do not work well in early morning classes.

questions.stem: What can we learn about Kivalina?

options.C: It is fighting for one last hope.
options.D: It has given up its chance to rise.

questions.passage:
Kivalina, an island village in Alaska, USA, is known by only a few people. It cannot be found on most maps of Alaska because it is only 10 km2. This small Arctic village is home to 400 Inuit people*. However, their home will become uninhabitable because the island could be covered by the Chukchi Sea by 2025. These Inuit people will have to leave their home.
But life now is already difficult. Over the past twenty years, winters have become warmer, the Arctic ice has kept melting, and the sea has been rising. These changes have made it harder to live by hunting and fishing than before. What's worse, there is no Arctic ice thick enough to keep them safe from terrible wind and rain.
These Inuit people think it is the oil and power companies in Alaska that have brought all these troubles, but they are the ones who are paying the price. It would cost hundreds of millions to move their village, and they have no idea where to get the money. Groups like ReLocate are working together with the Inuit people to save Kivalina, but nobody is sure if their hard work will come to anything. All the Inuit people can do now is to pray the rising sea will not cover their home too soon.

* The Inuit people are a group of people who live in the Arctic.

(melt 融化　hunt 打獵　company 公司)
questions.explain: 村莊面臨被海淹沒的危機，仍有團體與族人努力搶救、祈禱，屬於為最後希望奮鬥，選 C。

questions.stem: But before you decide whether it's OK to have such a long commute, it's best to know ______ .

options.B: how you can enjoy it more
options.C: how it will change your life
options.D: what is the best way of commute for you

questions.passage:
After a lot of tests and interviews, you finally got into a good school or got a good job. But there's a problem: it's a long way from home. “Well, I can take the bus or drive,” you might think. But before you decide whether it's OK to have such a long commute, it's best to know 35 .
Having a long commute means you'll get fewer hours of sleep, and that'll make you feel tired easily. You'll also have less time for friends and family, for exercise, or for a nice meal.
A long commute not only eats up your time but also 36 . Studies show that people who have a long commute get fat easily and often have neck or back problems. It's easier for them to have heart problems too. 37 . Studies find that these people get angry more often, feel less happy with their lives, and do less well at work.
Now, does that good school or that good job still sound good to you?

(interview 面試　commute 通勤)
questions.explain: 下文列舉長途通勤對睡眠、健康、心情等生活各層面的影響，故空格應為「它會如何改變你的生活」，選 C。

questions.stem: To the cities that fight for this chance, ______ . The Olympics usually do a lot of good to the host city.

options.B: this sounds like a good idea
options.C: this is perhaps a smart answer
options.D: this is old news they have heard

questions.passage:
NOlympics in Munich
11/11/2013

People in the city of Munich have spoken their mind: they do not want to host the Winter Olympics in 2022. To the cities that fight for this chance, 38 . The Olympics usually do a lot of good to the host city. The Games bring in jobs, visitors, and, most important of all, money. So why did people in Munich say no?
The Olympics 39 for people in Munich. For them, the world's biggest sports festival could invite serious trouble. There would be a lot of building work before the Games, and during the Games, too much traffic and too many visitors. Life would become terrible and nature would be hurt. And 40 : Munich hosted the 1972 Summer Olympics, and its neighbor, the mountain town Garmisch-Partenkirchen, hosted the 1936 Winter Olympics.
Still, the heavy price Munich paid does not worry the cities that are in the race to get the 2022 Winter Olympics. 41 , they are happy that Munich said no to the Olympics—their chances of getting the Games have become higher.

(host 舉辦　Olympics 奧運　visitor 遊客)
questions.explain: 奧運通常帶來就業、遊客與金錢，對爭辦的城市而言，慕尼黑說不肯定是意外之事，選 A。

questions.stem: Look at the picture. The mother duck is ______ the baby ducks across the street.
questions.explain: 母鴨帶著小鴨過馬路，用 lead（帶領）最貼切。carry 是抱著、push 是推、watch 是看。

questions.stem: Ms. Liu usually gives us a lot of homework, ______ she didn’t give us any today.
questions.explain: 前後語意相反（平常出很多、今天沒出），需用轉折連接詞 but。

questions.stem: Playing games on the cellphone ______ popular with high school students.
questions.explain: 動名詞片語 Playing games 當主詞視為單數，須搭配 is。

questions.stem: There ______ more than twenty clubs in our school. Which one would you like to join?
questions.explain: There be 句型，主詞 clubs 為複數，用 are。

questions.stem: Diane had a bad cold last week. She is still too ______ to go mountain climbing with you.
questions.explain: 重感冒後身體虛弱不能爬山，用 weak（虛弱）最合理。

questions.stem: The book under Mary’s desk is ______ , but I don’t know why it is there.
questions.explain: be 動詞後需用所有格代名詞 mine（我的東西）。my 後須接名詞。

questions.stem: Last night a garbage truck hit a tree and turned over. Now the street is ______ with lots of garbage.
questions.explain: 垃圾車翻覆，街道滿是垃圾，用 dirty（髒）最貼切。

questions.stem: It almost killed Kevin to take care of his baby sister for one afternoon. He wondered how his mom could do ______ every day.
questions.explain: it 代替前面「照顧妹妹」這件事，是唯一單數且指整件事的代名詞。

questions.stem: Joyce tells her brother everything. She never ______ anything from him.
questions.explain: 她什麼都跟哥哥說，從不對他隱瞞，用 hide（隱藏）。hide something from someone 為固定用法。

questions.stem: Ben always uses his birthday as a(n) ______ to ask Dad to buy him something expensive.
questions.explain: 拿生日當「藉口」要爸爸買貴的東西，用 excuse。

questions.stem: ______ that last piece of pie? If not, can I have it? I didn’t eat much this morning.
questions.explain: 問對方「打不打算吃」最後一塊派，用未來意圖 be going to。

questions.stem: Everyone in the class enjoys playing chess ______ Bill; he thinks the game is boring.
questions.explain: 全班都愛下棋「除了」Bill，用 except（除…之外）。

questions.stem: In my school days, I ______ to English radio programs every day. That was how I learned English at that time.
questions.explain: 描述過去的習慣（求學時期每天聽），用 used to（過去常常）。

questions.stem: Tom’s voice ______ when he talks about his neighbors. He speaks faster, his face becomes redder and you can almost see fire in his eyes.
questions.explain: 他愈說愈激動、聲音提高，用 rise（升高）。

questions.stem: ______ other waiters in the restaurant have worked here longer than Clark; only Lois and Lana started working here before him.
questions.explain: 只有兩人比 Clark 早來，所以「很少」服務生比他資深，用 Few。

questions.stem: What are “Daddy and me” doing in the poem?
questions.explain: 詩中在做 goulash（燉牛肉湯），刀切牛肉、馬鈴薯下鍋，是在烹飪。歌唱與跳舞只是比喻。
questions.passage:
Making goulash
we don’t talk much,
Daddy and me.

“The talking’s in the making, son,”
he smiles.
I see what he means.

It’s in the knife
meeting the beef,
in the dancing of potatoes
as they turn in the water,
in the singing
of boiling soup in the pot.

There are enough words
in the way
Daddy teaches me his famous dish
and the way he hugs me
when we finish.

Making goulash
we don’t talk much,
Daddy and me,

but everything is said.

(Ideas from Candace Pearson’s poem)

questions.stem: What can we most likely know about the speaker?

options.B: He enjoys his time with his father.
options.C: He often makes goulash for his father.
options.D: He wants to be as famous as his father.

questions.explain: 父子一起做菜、擁抱，「盡在不言中」，說話者享受與父親相處的時光。

questions.stem: Which is true about City Sight Bus tickets?

options.A: It costs less to buy tickets on the Internet.
options.B: People must pay the full ticket price for a pet.
options.C: Tickets are more expensive in the high season.
options.D: People save 10% if they buy two tickets for the same line.

questions.explain: 海報寫「Save 10% if you buy tickets on the Internet.」網路購票便宜 10%，故 A 正確。寵物半價、同線兩張無折扣、票價不因旺季調整。
questions.passage:
City Sight Bus (www.citysightbus.org)
[路線圖：停靠站含 Central Station、Farmers’ Bridge、Queen Marguerite’s Park、East End Temple、Westside Church、Starlight Garden、Great Age Theater、Grand Castle of King Charles、King Henry’s Castle、King Henry’s Garden、Lovers’ Bridge、City Square、King James’ Castle 等]

OPENING HOURS
High Season (April to September): 9:00 – 18:00 every day
Low Season (October to March): 9:00 – 17:00 every day

TICKET PRICES
Line A: $20 each person
Line B: $18 each person

THINGS YOU SHOULD KNOW
Buses for both lines leave every hour from Central Station.
Line A takes one hour; Line B takes 50 minutes.
If you are taking your pet with you, please buy a seat for it at half price.
Save 10% if you buy tickets on the Internet.
Save 10% if you buy tickets for both lines.
Go to www.citysightbus.org for more information.

questions.stem: Which is true about the bus lines?

options.C: Both lines leave from Central Station.
options.D: There are more gardens to see on Line A.

questions.explain: 海報明寫「Buses for both lines leave every hour from Central Station.」兩線皆從中央站發車，故 C 正確。Line B 只需 50 分鐘較快。

questions.stem: Why were there seventeen calls asking for Mike?

options.A: His team was angry at him.
options.B: He is a popular soccer player.
options.C: He did not show up for the game on Friday.
options.D: His name did not appear on the list of players.

questions.explain: Mike 忘了拿球隊球衣，隊友（Johnny、Ricky 等）打了 17 通電話罵他「你死定了」，是全隊在生氣。
questions.passage:
Sarah: You’re VERY popular today. I’ve had seventeen calls asking for you.
Mike: Finally! After all these years of kicking and running, I’ve got my own fans! So what did they want? My photos? Do I need to sign my name? Are they starting a fan club for me?
Sarah: Well, let me ask you this: What day was yesterday?
Mike: Friday… Why?
Sarah: Did you forget something?
Mike: Did I forget… OH, NO! I didn’t pick up my soccer team’s sport shirts from the shop! And we’re having this big game tomorrow!
Sarah: Yeah. And from what I’ve heard, it’s your team’s most important game this season.
Mike: OK. I have to go fix this now.
Sarah: Too late. They’ve tried. It’s not open on the weekend. And if you want to know, they are Johnny, Ricky, Archie, Freddie…
Mike: I know, I know, everyone on my team. Oh, I’m a dead man now.
Sarah: Yeah, that was what they said on the phone, seventeen times.

questions.stem: What does It mean in the dialogue?
questions.explain: 「It’s not open on the weekend.」週末沒開的是那家店（shop），指前文取球衣的店。

questions.stem: Which is the best title for this reading?

options.A: Australia: The Best Place for Birds.
options.C: Larger Mother Birds Have Larger Eggs.
options.D: Family Love: Brothers & Sisters Work Together.

questions.explain: 全文重點是母鳥依天氣好壞調整蛋的大小以對抗惡劣環境，故標題選「母鳥對抗天氣變化」。
questions.passage:
Animals have their special ways to deal with hard times in nature. The superb fairy-wren, one kind of bright-blue bird in Australia, is one example. This year, a ten-year study showed that this small bird has eggs of different sizes in different kinds of weather. When the weather is hot and dry, there is less food for young superb fairy-wrens, and they die easily. So the mother bird will make larger eggs to help her babies grow stronger inside before they break out of the eggs into the “hungry” world.

However, not all mother birds are able to do this trick. Only those with at least one male child can. When the weather is “good,” the mother will make smaller eggs, and her sons will bring food back for their baby brothers and sisters from the eggs. With her sons’ help, the mother can save more energy to make larger eggs when the weather is bad. Isn’t this amazing?

questions.stem: What does this trick mean in the reading?

options.C: Saving food for weaker baby birds.
options.D: Helping baby birds break out of the eggs.

questions.explain: this trick 指前段所述母鳥「依天氣下不同大小的蛋」這項本領。

questions.stem: What can we learn about superb fairy-wrens from the reading?

options.A: Male birds can usually live for ten years.
options.B: Baby birds grow well in hot, dry weather.
options.C: Young male birds share the job of feeding the baby birds.
options.D: Mother birds make smaller eggs when there is less food outside.

questions.explain: 文中說天氣好時母鳥下小蛋，兒子們會幫忙帶食物回來餵弟妹，故 C 正確。

questions.stem: What idea is talked about in both pieces of news?

options.A: More young people need to join in making art.
options.B: People’s ways of enjoying art change with time.
options.C: Bringing important art works to Taiwan from abroad.
options.D: Giving new life to old arts that were almost forgotten.

questions.explain: 紙紮（快消失）與相聲（曾式微）兩則都在講為幾乎被遺忘的老藝術注入新生命。
questions.passage:
Here are two pieces of news that Annie collected for her report.

Papier-mâché is works of art that are made of paper. In Taiwan, it is known as zhizha and is usually burned as a gift for dead people. Taiwan’s papier-mâché is dying because it is not much needed now. However, this dying art won a Frenchman’s heart when he visited the island. He then invited one of the papier-mâché shops, Hsin Hsin Paper Offering Store, to show their beautiful works in France. The shop owner’s son also used papier-mâché in a way that no one ever thought of—making a movie with it.

Stan Lai is very important in Taiwan’s theater. He brought changes to Chinese theater art of the old days to make it closer to our life experiences and easier to understand. One of his most famous works is That Evening, We Performed Crosstalk (1985). It was such a smash hit that people became interested again in xiangsheng, a show of funny talk between two actors. This art used to be a hobby that only a few people shared, but Lai has opened a bigger market for it. Now it has fans across all ages.

questions.stem: What does it mean when something is a smash hit?
questions.explain: 由後文「讓人們重新對相聲感興趣、打開更大市場」可推知 smash hit 指「大成功、大受歡迎」。

questions.stem: What do we know from the news?

options.A: Lai has taught xiangsheng in many schools.
options.B: Lai often puts his own life stories into his art works.
options.C: The owner of Hsin Hsin Paper Offering Store is a Frenchman.
options.D: People do not burn papier-mâché for dead people as often as before.

questions.explain: 文中說紙紮因「現在需求不多而漸漸沒落」，可推知焚燒紙紮的需求已不如從前。

questions.stem: Which idea is talked about in the news?

options.A: How often we should drink coffee.
options.B: What happens when we have too much coffee.
options.C: How to help the body make the cortisol we need.
options.D: What happens when we have coffee at the wrong time.

questions.explain: 全文重點在「剛起床（皮質醇最高時）喝咖啡是最糟時機」，談的是喝錯時間會怎樣。
questions.passage:
VOF News  16/01/2015

For many people, having a cup of coffee right out of bed is the best way to start a day. But a report from Asap Science says otherwise. It says the worst time for coffee is soon after we get up. When we wake up, our bodies start to make cortisol. Cortisol helps us to think clearly and be quick to understand and act. The cortisol levels rise after we wake up and climb to the highest in about an hour. So there are two problems when we drink coffee during this time: (1) our bodies will make less cortisol, and (2) the cortisol will make coffee less useful. And that’s why some of us drink more and more coffee.

Then, when’s the best time to have coffee? When the cortisol levels are going down, the report says. Cortisol levels are usually highest between 8 a.m. and 9 a.m., between noon and 1 p.m., and between 5:30 p.m. and 6:30 p.m. So if you want to have coffee, enjoy it after these times, and this popular drink will help you the most.

questions.stem: What does it mean when someone thinks otherwise about something?

options.B: They have no idea about it.
options.D: They do not think it is a problem.

questions.explain: 報告與一般人「起床喝咖啡最好」的看法相反，故 otherwise 指「有不同的看法」。

questions.stem:
Four people read the news and wrote what they thought about it.
Mike (Jan 16 8:26 pm): The best time to drink coffee is when you want to drink coffee.
Sarah (Jan 16 8:43 pm): I usually wake up AFTER I drink coffee!
Charlie (Jan 16 8:59 pm): The worst time to drink coffee is when you're DEAD.
Lisa (Jan 16 9:18 pm): If you need someone to tell you when to drink coffee, well, that’s just sad.
What did they think about the news?

options.A: They wanted to try what it said.
options.B: They were sad about what it said.
options.C: They did not agree with what it said.
options.D: They were not surprised at what it said.

questions.explain: 四則留言都用調侃口吻反駁報告（想喝就喝、需要別人告訴你才可悲），顯示他們不認同新聞說法。
questions.passage:
VOF News  16/01/2015

For many people, having a cup of coffee right out of bed is the best way to start a day. But a report from Asap Science says otherwise. It says the worst time for coffee is soon after we get up. When we wake up, our bodies start to make cortisol. Cortisol helps us to think clearly and be quick to understand and act. The cortisol levels rise after we wake up and climb to the highest in about an hour. So there are two problems when we drink coffee during this time: (1) our bodies will make less cortisol, and (2) the cortisol will make coffee less useful. And that’s why some of us drink more and more coffee.

Then, when’s the best time to have coffee? When the cortisol levels are going down, the report says. Cortisol levels are usually highest between 8 a.m. and 9 a.m., between noon and 1 p.m., and between 5:30 p.m. and 6:30 p.m. So if you want to have coffee, enjoy it after these times, and this popular drink will help you the most.

questions.stem: What does the report NOT suggest?

options.A: Having more free buses for shoppers.
options.B: Changing the opening hours on weekdays.
options.C: Making plans to help weekday morning business.
options.D: Changing the time of the special sale on weekends.

questions.explain: 報告建議增加接駁車、延長平日營業、更改週末特賣時間，但未提出改善「平日上午」業績的計畫。
questions.passage:
Below is part of a new shopping center’s market report.

Our busiest time is weekend afternoons, between 1:30 p.m. and 3:30 p.m. About half the business of the week is done then. So it would be better if we move Weekend’s Best Buy from Saturday evening to Saturday afternoon when there may be more shoppers during the sale. We should also think about having more free buses during the weekend, as 75% of the shoppers who were interviewed said two buses an hour are just not enough.

Weekend evenings are second busiest, though it is not as good as last season. An interesting fact is that business is better on weekday evenings, usually one hour before closing, than on weekend mornings. Most of the shoppers then are people who live near or working parents who do their last-minute shopping. They together make up almost 60% of the business on weekday evenings. It might bring in more business if we open half an hour longer during weekdays, until 10:30 p.m.

questions.stem: What does the report say about the shopping center?

options.A: Its business on weekdays is not as good as last season.
options.B: It has a special sale one hour before closing every day.
options.C: Most of the shoppers take the free buses to the shopping center.
options.D: A large part of its shoppers on weekday evenings are working parents.

questions.explain: 報告說平日晚上顧客多為住附近的人與趕著採買的上班族父母，兩者合占近六成，故 D 正確。

questions.stem: It hurts me to think that you’re going to ______ .
questions.explain: 後文說 Maggie 想像大眼名伶般改變外貌、且提到 this change，可知空格指「改變原本的自己」。
questions.passage:
Dear Maggie,

I know you won’t be happy but I still have to say this again. It hurts me to think that you’re going to ______(36). I know you’ve always wanted to be like those actresses with big eyes and slim legs, but in fact you are already beautiful. What you are born with makes you special because nobody looks the same as you.

It worries me more that ______(37). But sadly, there’s no quick fix to this, not even with the doctor’s help. If you don’t try to like yourself first, no one else will. People like you not because of your face, but because of your heart. You need to understand this first. Until you understand this, nothing is going to be different even if you have this change.

I truly hope you think this through again.

Love,
Linda

questions.stem: It worries me more that ______ .

options.A: you’re too shy to talk with people
options.B: you just don’t want to be like others
options.C: you don’t think you’re good enough
options.D: you don’t know what you’ve missed

questions.explain: 後文勸她要先喜歡自己、了解別人是因她的心而愛她，可推知讓 Linda 更擔心的是 Maggie「覺得自己不夠好」。

questions.stem: No one thought one day she ______ the queen of the UK.
questions.explain: 以過去時間為基準，敘述「當時沒人想到她日後會成為女王」，用過去未來式 would become。
questions.passage:
Elizabeth Alexandra Mary Windsor was born in London on April 21, 1926. No one thought one day she ______(38) the queen of the UK. After her grandfather, King George V, died in 1936, Elizabeth’s uncle, Prince Edward, became King Edward VIII. Edward ______(39) in love with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry her after he became the king. However, Edward was asked to choose between the throne and his love. Finally, he decided to give up the throne.

______(40). After Edward VIII gave up the throne, Elizabeth’s father became King George VI. As the oldest child without any brothers, Elizabeth became next in line to the throne. She needed to say goodbye to her good old life and prepare to be a queen. After her father died in 1952, she became Queen Elizabeth II.

Ever since she became head of her country, Queen Elizabeth II ______(41): She has held meetings with important people in the UK, and has visited many foreign countries. In the few hours she keeps to herself, however, she reads and enjoys horseback riding.

questions.stem: Edward ______ in love with a married American woman long before, and he decided to marry her after he became the king.
questions.explain: 「愛上」發生在成為國王（過去）之前的更早（long before），用過去完成式 had fallen。

questions.explain: 頭髮一直被吹起，代表風很強，故選 wind（風）。

questions.stem: When the baby cried, Mr. Wu ______ in the kitchen and did not hear it.
questions.explain: 寶寶哭的那一刻正在廚房煮飯，過去進行式表當時正在進行的動作，故選 was cooking。

questions.stem: I don't have any money with me because I ______ my wallet this morning.
questions.explain: today morning 是明確過去時間點，用簡單過去式 lost 表已完成的單一動作。

questions.stem: You need not only good luck but also hard work to become a ______ baseball player.
questions.explain: 需要運氣加努力才能成為「成功的」棒球選手，故選 successful。

questions.stem: The woman who lives next door to Ken is a ______ to him. They have never met and he doesn't even know her name.
questions.explain: 從沒見過面、連名字都不知道，代表是陌生人，故選 stranger。

questions.stem: With the new bus line, it is much more ______ for Fanny to go to school. It saves her a lot of time now.
questions.explain: 新公車路線省下很多時間，代表上學更「方便」，故選 convenient。

questions.stem:
Dad: Have you brushed your teeth yet?
Ann: No, but I ______ before I go to bed.
questions.explain: 還沒刷，但睡前「會」刷，表未來意願用 will。

questions.stem:
Alice: Do you know ______ Grandma is going to visit us?
Willy: It's next week. She told me on the phone. And we'll take her to the national park.
questions.explain: 回答是「下星期」，問的是時間，名詞子句引導詞用 when。

questions.stem: It took the police lots of time ______ who entered Liu's house and killed her one year ago.
questions.explain: It takes + 時間 + to V 句型，表花時間去查明，故選 to find out。

questions.stem: Many famous people visit this restaurant. Popular singers like A-mei and Jay Chou ______ here with their friends.
questions.explain: 歌手是「被看到」，需用被動語態 are often seen，且句子需要主要動詞。

questions.stem: The factory has been throwing trash into the river for years. Now ______ must be taken to stop this.
questions.explain: take action 為固定搭配，表採取行動制止，故選 action。

questions.stem: The beach party tonight ______ the end of our summer vacation. Tomorrow we are all going back home and getting ready for school.
questions.explain: 海灘派對「標誌」暑假結束，mark 有標記、象徵之意，故選 marks。

questions.stem: Anyone who reads Loren's business plan will quickly see some serious problems in it, or ______ feel something is not right.
questions.explain: 就算沒看出問題，「至少」也會覺得哪裡不對，故選 at least。

questions.stem: I've wanted to read The Diary of a Young Girl for months, ______ today I finally borrowed the book from the library.
questions.explain: 前後兩件事順接（一直想讀、今天終於借到），用對等連接詞 and。

questions.stem: 選出第 16 格最適合的答案。
questions.passage:
Robert likes to take a walk in the park after work. One day when he was walking in the park, he heard a woman calling his name. He stopped to look around, but didn't see anything __(16)__. Shortly after Robert saw it, it was gone.
      Though Robert felt a little strange, he did not think about it too much and sat down for a rest on a bench in the park. Then he noticed the snake he had just seen __(17)__. Robert was too afraid to move away from the bench. Right at this moment, a woman behind him shouted, "Come here, Robert. You can't stay on the bench like that. __(18)__."
      In surprise, Robert turned around and said to the woman, "Excuse me, but that's not a very nice thing to say, and in fact some people say I'm handsome."
      "I'm not talking to you," said the woman. "I'm telling Robert, my pet snake, to get down from the bench, not you."
questions.explain: 下句提到 the snake he had just seen，可知看到的是一條小蛇，故選 except a little snake。

questions.stem: 選出第 19 格最適合的答案。

options.C: to make itself a famous city

questions.passage:
If you cannot live without your car, Zurich might be the last city you would like to visit. In Zurich, people are welcome, but cars are not! Over the past 20 years, this city has used smart ways __(19)__. One is to keep the same total number of parking spaces. For example, if 50 new parking spaces are built in one part of the city, then 50 old spaces in other parts are taken away for other uses. So the total number does not change. Some are unhappy that there are never enough spaces. That is just what the city has in mind: If people find parking more difficult, they will drive less.
      __(20)__, the total number of cars in the city is counted. Over 3,500 little computers are put under Zurich roads to check the number of cars that enter the city. If the number is higher than the city can deal with, the traffic lights on the roads that enter the city will be kept red. So drivers who are traveling into Zurich have to stop and wait until there are fewer cars in the city. Now, you may wonder __(21)__. The answer is simple: The city wants to make more space for its people.
questions.explain: 全文在講蘇黎世用各種方法減少車流，故選 to make traffic lighter（讓交通變順暢／車少）。

questions.stem: What is the reading mostly about?

options.A: Where to find a nice holiday apartment.
options.B: How to choose a nice holiday apartment.
options.C: Why one should choose a holiday apartment.
options.D: How one can change their house into a holiday apartment.

questions.passage:
Edward's Travel Tips
         What kind of place do you usually choose to stay for the night during a trip? Expensive hotels that may make you feel like you don't get what you pay for? Or cheap hostels that put you and five other strangers in the same bedroom? If both choices sound terrible to you, here's something new: holiday apartments.
         In a holiday apartment, you have more space than what a hotel room can give you. Everything in the apartment is at your disposal. The living room, the kitchen, the study, and, of course, the bedrooms are all for your own use. Some apartments even have a lovely garden or a game room. You'll feel at home in the holiday apartment. The best thing is, a holiday apartment is not expensive. The price for a holiday apartment is for two people, and the apartment is often cheaper than a hotel room for two. You only need to pay a little more for a third or fourth person. If you travel with friends or your family, a holiday apartment will be your best choice!
(hostel：提供廉價食宿的旅舍)
questions.explain: 全文列舉度假公寓的種種優點來說服讀者選擇它，主旨是「為何該選度假公寓」，故選 C。

questions.stem: From the reading, what can we learn about holiday apartments?

options.A: They are good for people who like to take one-day trips.
options.B: They are good for people who travel in a group of three or more.
options.C: They are good for people who want to make new friends when traveling.
options.D: They are good for people who like to spend their holidays in the country.

options.A: You are free to use it.
options.D: You can buy it at a lower price.

questions.stem: What is the quick lesson of SHARP WEAR about?

options.A: How to get to one of SHARP WEAR's stores.
options.B: How to send back a T-shirt to SHARP WEAR.
options.C: How to shop for a T-shirt from SHARP WEAR.
options.D: How to take care of a T-shirt from SHARP WEAR.

questions.passage:
SHARP WEAR: A T-shirt like no other!
               First time here? Don't worry! Here is our quick lesson for you!
1. What kind of T-shirt do you like? (4 kinds of T-shirts to choose from!)
2. What color for the T-shirt? (We have as many as 16 colors!)
3. Want a picture on your T-shirt? No, thank you. / Oh yes! (Now choose from one of our 72 pictures or send us your own!)
4. Pocket? No pocket? I don't need one. / Of course! What kind of pocket then? (We've got 4 shapes for you!)
5. How about a different color for the pocket? No, thank you. Same color as the T-shirt. / Why not? (Choose from the 16 colors AGAIN!)
6. Your size? (7 sizes for you!)
7. How many T-shirts do you want?
8. Finally, you can pay!
questions.explain: 整份說明是一步步教你如何客製並下單買一件 T 恤，故選 How to shop for a T-shirt。

questions.stem: What can we learn about SHARP WEAR?

options.A: You have to order at least four T-shirts each time.
options.B: You can choose from sixteen colors for both the T-shirt and the pocket.
options.C: Some of the T-shirts have pictures on both the front side and the back side.
options.D: T-shirts in the wrong sizes cannot be sent back if they have pictures or pockets on them.

questions.passage:
SHARP WEAR: A T-shirt like no other!
               First time here? Don't worry! Here is our quick lesson for you!
1. What kind of T-shirt do you like? (4 kinds of T-shirts to choose from!)
2. What color for the T-shirt? (We have as many as 16 colors!)
3. Want a picture on your T-shirt? No, thank you. / Oh yes! (Now choose from one of our 72 pictures or send us your own!)
4. Pocket? No pocket? I don't need one. / Of course! What kind of pocket then? (We've got 4 shapes for you!)
5. How about a different color for the pocket? No, thank you. Same color as the T-shirt. / Why not? (Choose from the 16 colors AGAIN!)
6. Your size? (7 sizes for you!)
7. How many T-shirts do you want?
8. Finally, you can pay!
questions.explain: 步驟 2 T 恤有 16 色、步驟 5 口袋也從同樣 16 色再選一次，故 T 恤與口袋都可從 16 色中挑，選 B。

questions.stem: Which season is this poem about?
questions.passage:
The sky is turning bright and clear.
The earth is wearing red and green.
Winter is gone; spring has come.
The air smells fresh and sweet.

The summer heat is not yet here;
A little fox comes out to play.
A bird is busy in a tree
Waking a daisy from her sleep.

Little daisy, little daisy,
People call the flower the "day's eye."
When she opens her eye,
The springtime brings us cheer.
(poem：詩)
questions.explain: 詩中明說 Winter is gone; spring has come、summer heat is not yet here，並以 springtime 作結，故是春天，選 A。

questions.stem: What is a daisy?
questions.passage:
The sky is turning bright and clear.
The earth is wearing red and green.
Winter is gone; spring has come.
The air smells fresh and sweet.

The summer heat is not yet here;
A little fox comes out to play.
A bird is busy in a tree
Waking a daisy from her sleep.

Little daisy, little daisy,
People call the flower the "day's eye."
When she opens her eye,
The springtime brings us cheer.
(poem：詩)
questions.explain: 詩中 People call the flower the "day's eye" 直接說 daisy 是花，故選 A kind of flower。

questions.stem: What does the reading say about shaking?

options.A: Different animals' shaking begins with different body parts.
options.B: Shaking is a way that animals use to make other animals scared.
options.C: Animals that are not kept as pets do the shaking better than those that are.
options.D: Shaking themselves dry may help animals run faster in dangerous moments.

questions.passage:
When we get wet, we need a towel to get ourselves dry. When a dog gets wet, all it needs is to shake its body. A study in 2010 showed that a wet dog can throw off half the water on its body by shaking for less than a second. In fact, this common act of dogs works better than a washing machine.
      The study found that animal shaking begins with the head and ends with the tail. During a shake, the animal's head, body and skin all move. Smaller animals must shake faster than bigger animals to get water off. For example, in one second, a rat can shake 18 times, a dog 6 times, and a bear 4 times. Bigger animals can get their bodies dry with fewer shakes.
      For animals, shaking is not just about getting themselves dry. It is also about saving their lives. Being wet makes animals heavier, and that makes it harder to run. In the animal world, how fast an animal can run often decides whether it will live or not. Maybe that's why the "wet-dog shake" has become a common habit of many animals.
(skin：皮膚)
questions.explain: 末段說濕會變重、跑不快，甩乾能救命，可推知甩乾有助危急時跑更快，選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the reading?

options.A: The animal's tail can help it run more quickly and easily.
options.B: Some animals shake more than they need to get water off.
options.C: The idea of washing machines came from the shaking of dogs.
options.D: Larger animals get themselves drier at each shake than smaller animals.

questions.stem: What does things like that mean?

options.A: Problems about the free coffee plan.
options.B: Trouble for people who go to Flora Café.
options.C: Ways to make the free coffee plan successful.
options.D: Good things free coffee will bring to Flora Café.

questions.passage:
Josh: Do you know about "pay it forward coffee" at Flora Café?
Eric: You mean you pay for a cup of coffee for someone poor to have it later?
Josh: Yeah. A cup of coffee is not much, but on cold winter days like today, it might warm them up a little. And maybe their hearts too.
Eric: But will Flora Café really give this cup of coffee to someone later?
Josh: Come on. I've known the shopkeeper well. He's an honest man. He's got a blackboard in the shop that says how many cups are paid for and how many have been given out.
Eric: But how will they know who to give? Anyone can ask for it, even if they're not poor.
Josh: True, but then I guess they'll just have to believe in people.
Eric: But will poor people go and ask for a free coffee? Won't they worry about losing face?
Josh: Why do you always say things like that?
Eric: Well, it may happen.
Josh: Yeah, I know, but I still think it's a good thing to do, and it gets people to care about others.
questions.explain: Eric 一直提出這計畫的種種問題（誰能領、窮人會不會不好意思），things like that 指這些問題，選 A。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Josh and Eric?

options.A: Josh was not able to change Eric's mind about free coffee.
options.B: Eric has more hope for the free coffee plan than Josh does.
options.C: They found a way to make people feel OK to take free coffee.
options.D: They do not know whether Flora Café will do business honestly.

questions.explain: Josh 力挺此計畫，Eric 始終質疑，到最後 Eric 仍說 it may happen，可見 Josh 沒能改變 Eric 想法，選 A。

questions.stem: What do we know about Flora Café from the dialogue?

options.A: It has a way to know who really is poor.
options.B: Free coffee has brought it more business.
options.C: People pay less for free coffee than other coffee.
options.D: Its blackboard shows if there are free coffees to be taken.

questions.explain: Josh 說店裡有黑板記錄付了幾杯、送出幾杯，可知黑板顯示還有沒有免費咖啡可領，選 D。

questions.stem:
Below are the writer's points in the reading:
   a. Whether we should stop tribespeople whaling
   b. The problem of whaling
   c. Whaling as a way of life
In what order does the writer talk about his points?
questions.passage:
Over the years, the number of whales has dropped sharply. From 1946 to 1986, about 340,000 whales were killed. People worry that children in the future can only see whales in pictures. They believe all kinds of whaling (whale hunting) should be stopped before it's too late.
      Some people are trying to stop whaling all over the world. But they fail to notice one fact: Whaling was going on for a long time before the number of whales went down and became a problem.
      Whaling started as early as 1,500 years ago. This was how tribespeople fed their families. They hunted whales for meat because almost nothing could grow on their land. They also made whale fat into oil and used it to make candles or oil lamps. Over the years, whaling became their way of living, and even part of who they are.
      Tribe whaling is not the thing we should worry about. Of all the whales that were killed over the past forty years, only 10% were hunted by tribespeople. The other 90% died at the hands of the money-making whaling business. When we try to stop all kinds of whaling, we should think what we are asking tribespeople to give up and whether this is the best answer to the problem of whaling.
(tribe：部落 fat：脂肪)
questions.explain: 文章先談捕鯨造成鯨魚減少的問題(b)、再談捕鯨是部落的生活方式(c)、最後探討是否該阻止部落捕鯨(a)，順序為 b c a，選 B。

questions.stem: Which idea may the writer agree with?

options.A: We should stop all kinds of whale hunting.
options.B: We should not worry about the number of whales.
options.C: Tribespeople's way of living is as important as animal lives.
options.D: Working with the whaling business can make tribespeople's lives better.

questions.explain: 作者為部落捕鯨辯護，提醒禁捕會犧牲部落生活方式，暗示部落生活方式與動物生命同等重要，選 C。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the reading?

options.A: Tribespeople believe whales bring good luck.
options.B: Tribespeople become rich by selling whale oil.
options.C: Whaling was not a problem until 1,500 years ago.
options.D: Whaling helped tribespeople's lives in different ways.

questions.explain: 文中說部落捕鯨取肉餵養家人、鯨脂做油點燈製燭，可見捕鯨在多方面幫助部落生活，選 D。

questions.stem: From the news story, which fact is seen as a new turn?

options.A: Carolyn Cole is a first-time writer.
options.B: Carolyn Cole won the Inscriber Prize.
options.C: Carolyn Cole is not the winner's real name.
options.D: Carolyn Cole did not appear for the Inscriber Prize.

questions.passage:
NBN NEWS
         Ryan Kim 7/15/2013
         Guesses about Carolyn Cole have been flying around since last week, and now things have taken a new turn.
         Last week Carolyn Cole won the Inscriber Prize, the country's biggest book prize, with her book Paper Soldier. Cole herself did not show up for the prize. It was not only the first time in history that a first-time writer won the prize, but also the first time that the winner did not appear.
         Now here comes another "first time" for the Inscriber: Cole is not real. "She" is in fact Thomas Goode. Goode is known for his picture books. His best-known book, Blue Moon, won him two national book prizes and sold over one million copies.
         Goode had tried to write "serious" books before. His first try was Parent, which came out in 2006. Sadly, Goode's big name did not help, and there was little interest in Parent. Goode's friend told NBN that he wanted to write a book under a different name. Goode thought that people might treat his book seriously if they did not know it was from a picture book writer.
         For the moment, there has been no word from Goode yet.
questions.explain: 新的轉折是揭露 Carolyn Cole 並非真人、其實是 Thomas Goode，即 Cole 不是得主真名，選 C。

questions.stem: Which is most likely true about the Inscriber Prize?

options.A: It was started by NBN News.
options.B: It is a new prize for picture books.
options.C: It is a prize only for first-time writers.
options.D: It is treated seriously by writers in the country.

         For the moment, there has been no word from Goode yet.
questions.explain: 文中稱 Inscriber Prize 是全國最大書獎，且 Goode 想以此讓作品被認真看待，可知作家們相當重視此獎，選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Thomas Goode?

options.A: He has won the Inscriber Prize twice.
options.B: He told NBN that Paper Soldier was his work.
options.C: He wants to be more than just a picture book writer.
options.D: His life as a picture book writer has not been successful.

questions.stem: What do we know about Thomas Goode's books?

options.A: The sales of Parent were bad.
options.B: Paper Soldier is his best-selling book.
options.C: His first picture book came out in 2006.
options.D: Blue Moon was written under a different name.
