questions.stem: My sister wants to buy a car ______ she doesn't have enough money.
questions.explain: 前後兩句語意轉折（想買車，但錢不夠），要用表示轉折的對等連接詞 but。

questions.stem: Jim always forgets my birthday. He has asked me many times and still can't ______ it.
questions.explain: 前句說 Jim 老是忘記我的生日，問了很多次還是記不住，故選 remember（記得）。

questions.stem: The little girl was ______ by a truck on her way home and was badly hurt.
questions.explain: 被卡車撞（was hit by a truck）而受重傷，語意最合。hit 過去分詞同形。

questions.stem: Excuse me. I really need to go now. My kids ______ for me at school.
questions.explain: 「現在此刻正在等」用現在進行式 are waiting，配合 now 的情境。

questions.stem: Amy's father is very sick. The doctors can do nothing for him, so Amy went to the temple to ______ for him.
questions.explain: 到廟裡「為他祈禱」用 pray for，符合父親病重、醫生束手無策的情境。

questions.stem: I'm glad that my school has students wear ______. I don't have to worry about what clothes to wear to school.
questions.explain: 後句說不必煩惱穿什麼衣服上學，故學校規定穿的是制服 uniforms。

questions.stem: We are surprised that Sally and Ray decided to get married. No one knows ______. They often fight with each other and are unhappy together.
questions.explain: 兩人常吵架卻決定結婚，令人意外，沒人知道「為什麼」，故用 why 引導名詞子句。

questions.stem: I don't think Katie will read any one of the books you've picked out for her: ______ look(s) very boring.
questions.explain: 指前面複數的 books，且動詞為原形 look，故用複數代名詞 they。

questions.stem: Jenny's bag is very heavy because ______ filled with toy cars.
questions.explain: 主詞指單數的 bag，被玩具車裝滿用 it is filled with，故選 it is。

questions.stem: ______ a map with you when you go to a place for the first time.
questions.explain: 祈使句以原形動詞開頭，勸人第一次去某地要帶地圖，故用 Take。

questions.stem: The weather ______ rainy and cloudy in the last few days. I hope the sun will come out soon.
questions.explain: in the last few days（過去幾天到現在）搭配現在完成式 has been。

questions.stem: All the excuses Nick made ______ a lot about how much he hates to do the job.
questions.explain: 主詞 All the excuses（複數）需要主要動詞，故用原形 say。Nick made 為省略關係代名詞的修飾語。

questions.stem: Sabine walks so ______ in the apartment that sometimes you don't even know she's just passed by. You wonder if her feet ever touch the floor.
questions.explain: 走路輕到讓人幾乎察覺不到、像腳不著地，故選 lightly（輕盈地）。

questions.stem: In the past, people thought it was a ______ idea to send e-mails and read news online, but now it is part of our everyday life.
questions.explain: 過去覺得線上收信、看新聞是「新鮮（新奇）」的想法，如今習以為常，故選 fresh。

questions.stem: What is the book festival for?

options.B: Telling people how to choose books.
options.C: Inviting people to change books with each other.
options.D: Knowing what kinds of books are the most popular.

questions.passage:
Do you have some books you don't want? Your old love may be someone's new favorite. Bring one book to the Town Library & get another one home for free!
When & How:
March 2 - March 14   Bring one book for one book festival card.
March 16 - March 31   Bring one card for one book you like to read.
What books:
All kinds of books EXCEPT school books, comic books, and dictionaries.
Notice:
Your book must be in good shape, without any page missing.
Your book must be clean, and nothing is written on it.
Want to know more? Please call 1234-5678. (We are closed on Mondays.)
-Town Library-
questions.explain: 海報說帶一本書來換另一本回家，重點在讓大家彼此交換書，故選 C。

questions.stem: Sonja is busy packing some books for the book festival. Which is most likely one of them?

options.B: A book of short stories in English.
options.C: A picture book with her own notes.
options.D: A workbook she used in her math class.

questions.passage:
Do you have some books you don't want? Your old love may be someone's new favorite. Bring one book to the Town Library & get another one home for free!
When & How:
March 2 - March 14   Bring one book for one book festival card.
March 16 - March 31   Bring one card for one book you like to read.
What books:
All kinds of books EXCEPT school books, comic books, and dictionaries.
Notice:
Your book must be in good shape, without any page missing.
Your book must be clean, and nothing is written on it.
Want to know more? Please call 1234-5678. (We are closed on Mondays.)
-Town Library-
questions.explain: 規則排除字典、學校用書，且書上不可有字跡。A、C、D 皆違規，只有英文短篇故事集符合。

questions.stem: What happened in the story?

options.A: A killer was caught by the police.
options.B: The writer's family invited a stranger to dinner.
options.C: The writer's brother ran to their parents for help.
options.D: A man ran away after he heard the writer's mother's voice.

questions.passage:
I had a horrendous experience last Saturday. That day, my family went camping near a big lake. While my parents were preparing dinner, my brother and I were playing by the lake. A dirty man appeared from somewhere, and his face was half covered by his hair. He walked to us and asked for some water.
When I gave him water, the bag he carried dropped, and things inside fell out on the grass. I saw a rope, a knife, and a baseball bat. The strangest thing was that there were also a woman's shoe and a ring, and I'm sure they were not his. The man quickly put all his things back in the bag and looked at us angrily. At that moment, the picture of a man the police was looking for came to my mind. He was the crazy killer! I was so afraid that I could not move at all.
Luckily, before he could get any closer, my mom shouted from far away, and the man hurried off into the dark.
questions.explain: 文末母親遠處一喊，男子就匆匆逃進黑暗中，故選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about the writer and the man with a bag?

options.B: The writer followed the man into the dark.
options.C: The writer did not find out who the man was in the end.
options.D: The man did not want the writer to see things in his bag.

questions.passage:
I had a horrendous experience last Saturday. That day, my family went camping near a big lake. While my parents were preparing dinner, my brother and I were playing by the lake. A dirty man appeared from somewhere, and his face was half covered by his hair. He walked to us and asked for some water.
When I gave him water, the bag he carried dropped, and things inside fell out on the grass. I saw a rope, a knife, and a baseball bat. The strangest thing was that there were also a woman's shoe and a ring, and I'm sure they were not his. The man quickly put all his things back in the bag and looked at us angrily. At that moment, the picture of a man the police was looking for came to my mind. He was the crazy killer! I was so afraid that I could not move at all.
Luckily, before he could get any closer, my mom shouted from far away, and the man hurried off into the dark.
questions.explain: 袋內東西掉出後，男子迅速收回並憤怒瞪視，可見他不想被看到袋中物品，故選 D。

questions.stem: How do people feel when they experience something horrendous?

Luckily, before he could get any closer, my mom shouted from far away, and the man hurried off into the dark.
questions.explain: 作者嚇到動彈不得，可推知 horrendous（可怕的）經驗讓人感到害怕（scared）。

questions.stem: What does the catch mean in the dialogue?

options.A: A plan that starts well but ends badly.
options.B: A fact that everyone knows but never talks about.
options.C: A problem that is hidden behind something good.
options.D: An action that is right in one way but wrong in another.

questions.passage:
Ruth: I've got two tickets for a football game. The Lions vs. the Sharks, front-row seats, next Saturday. Interested? You can take Jessica to the game.
Nate: How did you get the tickets?! They were all sold out months ago!
Ruth: And a table for two at Leeds.
Nate: Leeds?! But I called them yesterday and they said they were all booked for the year!
Ruth: Now it's yours. You and Jessica can have a lovely dinner there after the game.
Nate: Wait a second. This is just not like you. What's the catch?
Ruth: Well, you know this Friday I'm leaving for my vacation in London, and . . . .
Nate: And what?
Ruth: And I was thinking if Charlie could stay with you next week.
Nate: The whole week?!
Ruth: Please! I can't just leave him in my apartment.
Nate: You know I hate that stupid dog! And he hates me too.
Ruth: Please! You're my last hope.
Nate: I need to think about it.
Ruth: OK, but not too long.
questions.explain: Ruth 給好處後才提出要 Nate 幫忙顧狗的條件，catch 指藏在好事背後的問題，故選 C。

questions.stem: What is this reading for?

options.A: Telling people rules about hospital visits.
options.B: Getting people to stand up and speak for doctors.
options.C: Telling people how to find a good doctor for themselves.
options.D: Getting doctors to share their experiences in saving lives.

questions.passage:
When you need to see a doctor, surely you will want to see a good one. But there is one important thing you may never have thought about: Has your doctor rested enough?
Doctors in our country often have to work 34 hours or longer without sleep. Most of them work 104 hours a week, and some even 150. (There are only 168 hours a week!) One report said that a doctor who works such long hours cannot think more clearly than a drunk driver. Another found that doctors who sleep 5.8 fewer hours than the week before might make 22% more serious mistakes. Surely no one wants to get in a car that is driven by a drunk driver. So, how safe would you feel when you are under the care of a tired doctor?
Sadly this problem has been around for a long time. There are no laws about it yet and hospital bosses don't look like they care either. And that is why we doctors are asking you to join us on the street this Sunday. Fight together with us on the street so we can fight better for your life in the hospital.
Time: 1 p.m. May 2nd    Place: City Hospital Square
questions.explain: 文末呼籲民眾週日上街與醫師一同抗爭，目的是號召大家為醫師發聲，故選 B。

questions.stem: What does this problem mean in the reading?

options.C: Doctors do not see enough people a day.
options.D: Doctors are not paid enough for their work.

questions.stem: What does Wonky Markt care about?

options.A: Supermarkets often sell "seconds" at a lower price.
options.B: Farmers don't know how to grow fewer "seconds."
options.C: "Seconds" are thrown away because of how they look.
options.D: People don't know much about picking fresh "seconds."

questions.passage:
Have you ever seen a pumpkin that looks like a nose or an apple that looks like a funny face? They may not look great, but they taste just as good as the pretty ones. These strange-looking fruit and vegetables are called "seconds." About 20-40% of what farmers grow is not so beautiful. Sadly, these "seconds" seldom arrive at your table, but often go straight into the trash can. Why? It's because supermarkets don't want to buy them from farmers-they believe their shoppers only love beautiful things. Because of these "seconds," some farmers lose a lot of money.
Wonky Markt wishes to change this. We're helping sell these "seconds." Farmers can save them from the trash can, and you can get them at a lower price.
1. NT$250 a box.
2. You can choose as many as six kinds of fruit and vegetables a box.
3. Service charge: NT$50 a box.
4. No service charge if order is over NT$1,000.
Send us photos of your delicious dishes that are made with our "seconds" to oneandonly@wonkymarkt.com, and you may win our cookbook.
questions.explain: 文章指醜蔬果因外觀被丟棄、農民因此虧損，Wonky Markt 想改變此事，故選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know about buying the "seconds" from Wonky Markt?

options.A: You can save 20-40% if you shop for a second time.
options.B: If you buy three boxes, you will need to pay NT$900.
options.C: You can choose not only their size but also their shape.
options.D: To win the cookbook, you need to buy at least six boxes.

questions.stem: What can we learn from the reading?

options.A: No buses go from Apple City to Orange Island before the bus line changes.
options.B: Before July 1, those who want to visit Mango Island have to take a bus at Berry City.
options.C: Traveling from Apple City to Grape Town will take less time after the bus line changes.
options.D: Before July 1, Dashing Shuttle does not have buses from Apple City to Peach City.

questions.passage:
Below is the news about Dashing Shuttle's bus line changes.
1. Great news for people who travel between Apple City and Peach City! From July 1, we have three more buses to Peach City every day.
2. A trip from Apple City to Lemon City or Berry City will take less time. From July 1, you can go from Apple City to each of the two cities without changing buses.
3. Dashing Shuttle is the first in the country to open a bus line between Apple City and Orange Island. From July 1, our bus will take you to Orange Island every day!
4. Our bus line between Apple City and Grape Town will stop running on June 30. From July 1, changing buses at Guava Town or Banana Town will be the best way to travel.
5. From July 1, our bus runs from Apple City to Mango Island three times a day. You don't need to change buses at Melon City anymore.
questions.explain: 第 3 點說 7/1 才首創蘋果市到橙島的路線，可知變更前沒有此線，故選 A。

questions.stem: What does the report say about globalization's influence on the movie world?

options.A: It has changed the way of making movies.
options.B: It has changed a country's domestic market for movies.
options.C: It has brought together movie makers from around the world.
options.D: It has helped US movies make more money abroad than in the US.

questions.passage:
Cinema Times, June 5th, 2005
Thanks to globalization, we get to see movies from around the world. If anyone should feel most excited about globalization, it is US movie makers. A study in 2001 shows that, for every 100 people who go to the theater, 85 see US movies. This may be good news for US movie makers, but not for those in other countries.
Chart 1 shows the percentages (%) of US movies in five countries in 1984 and in 2001; Chart 2 shows the percentages (%) of domestic movies in the five countries in 1984 and in 2001. In Chart 1, we can see that US movies have been popular in foreign markets. In Chart 2, we can see that domestic movies in the five countries are not doing well in their domestic market. The falling market for domestic movies may be a serious problem, but there is something more important. When people welcome US movies with open arms, they in fact also say yes to the American way of thinking. Some people in these countries worry that what is lost may not just be their domestic market for domestic movies, but also their own way of thinking that has been passed down from parents to children.
questions.explain: 報導藉圖表指出全球化使各國本土電影在自家市場節節敗退，即改變了一國電影的本土市場，故選 B。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the report?

options.A: The American way of thinking may be changed when it is brought to other countries.
options.B: More and more people are asking their country to help their domestic movie business.
options.C: US movies are popular in foreign markets because English is spoken in many countries.
options.D: The fact that US movies are popular may mean the American way of thinking is popular.

questions.stem: He ______ a proud businessman of a successful shaved ice shop.
questions.passage:
Jerry Stevens has been unhappy these months. He   34   a proud businessman of a successful shaved ice shop. His highest sales numbers were 1,899 plates a week; people called him "Ice King." But all this changed when the cookie shop across the street started selling ice cream cookies. It took away half of his business.
And   35   when Dan, his dead sister's only son, came to work for him.
Dan was a nice young man with a simple mind, too simple, maybe. He always let people try some shaved ice for free, but he never learned to see when "some" became "too much." Many people came, but few bought. When the sales did not grow with the heat, Jerry started to worry. He wanted to send Dan home. But how could he? Dan had no family except him.
Then, one day,   36  . And it was from across the street. The cookie shop   37   a clerk. For Jerry, it was a great chance to get Dan out of his store without sending him home. After taking the shopkeeper to several nice dinners, Jerry got Dan the job.
Now Jerry could finally try and save his store. And maybe Dan would help by sending him a few people who are thirsty from eating too many cookies.
questions.explain: 接著情況因對街餅乾店而改變，可知他「曾經是」風光的老闆，故用 used to be。

options.B: he was ready to give up

options.D: the cookie shop was not successful for long

questions.stem: The first one made him a star; the second ______.
questions.passage:
Englishman Robert Scott is known for leading two trips to Antarctica. The first one made him a star; the second   38  .
After his first successful trip, Scott decided to be the first person to stand on the South Pole. However,   39  . He would be in a race with Roald Amundsen, from Norway. Both left their countries by ship in June of 1910 and arrived in Antarctica in January of 1911.
About ten months later, both teams started their trips down to the South Pole.   40  . Amundsen's team used dogs, and Scott's team used horses. Because horses weren't good at traveling on snow, it took Scott's team 77 days to arrive at the South Pole. They got there on January 17, 1912, and were surprised to find that Amundsen was ahead of them. The news hit Scott very hard, but what he didn't know was that   41  . His team began their long trip home with broken hearts. After days of terrible weather and little food, Scott lost his men one after another, and he himself was the last one to meet the end of his life. No one on his team lived to go back home and tell their story. It was only learned through Scott's diary.
questions.explain: 後文說 Scott 一行人在返程中相繼喪命、他也殞命，第二趟讓他賠上性命，故選 C。

questions.stem:
Chris: Which ______ looks better on me?
Penny: I think purple's better. You look great in purple.
questions.explain: 由回答提到 purple（紫色）可知問的是「顏色」，故選 color。grade 年級、shape 形狀、size 尺寸皆不合語意。

questions.stem: My cat got excited when it saw the boy ______ the birds.
questions.explain: 感官動詞 see 後接受詞再接現在分詞，表示看見動作正在進行，故用 catching。

questions.stem: Mr. Jackson is a very ______ writer; people of all ages enjoy his stories.
questions.explain: 各年齡層都喜愛他的故事，代表他很「受歡迎」，故選 popular。polite 有禮、handsome 英俊、honest 誠實皆不符。

questions.stem: Carol sounded happy when we talked on the phone; I could feel joy in her ______ .
questions.explain: 講電話只能靠聲音判斷，從她的「聲音」感受到喜悅，故選 voice。

questions.stem: Mark studies very hard and never ______ classes. He goes to school even when he is sick.
questions.explain: 生病也去上學，代表他從不「缺（曠）課」，miss classes 為缺課的固定用法。

questions.stem: Have you got anything for Joe ______ ? He'd be happy to get your gift on his birthday.
questions.explain: 現在完成式的疑問句常用 yet（尚未、到目前為止）搭配，故選 yet。

questions.stem: Susan bought ______ bread in the supermarket, but she did not buy anything to drink.
questions.explain: bread 為不可數名詞，肯定句表「一些」用 some；many 修飾可數複數、any 多用於否定疑問，one 需接可數單數。

questions.stem: Playing sports at least three times a week ______ good for your health.
questions.explain: 主詞為動名詞片語 Playing sports，視為單數，且需 be 動詞接形容詞 good，故用 is。

questions.stem: My parents have different hobbies. One enjoys baking; ______ enjoys taking pictures.
questions.explain: 兩者中一個用 one，另一個（剩下唯一的）用 the other。父母共兩人，故選 the other。

questions.stem: My mom told me to take care of ______ in England. She worried that I couldn't eat or sleep well there.
questions.explain: told me 的 me 是被交代的人，照顧的對象即自己，主受詞同一人用反身代名詞 myself。

questions.stem: The paint on the wall is not as ______ as it was ten years ago. It has changed from white to gray.
questions.explain: 顏色由白轉灰，表示不再那麼「鮮亮」，故選 bright。

questions.stem: I told you this road went the wrong way, but you just wouldn't listen. Now we have to ______ and take another road.
questions.explain: 走錯路要「折返」再改走另一條路，turn back 為掉頭返回，符合語意。

questions.stem: The last five years have not been ______ to Jennie. Her face is covered with lines and she looks much older than she is.
questions.explain: be kind to 對某人好、善待。歲月「不曾善待」她，故臉上滿是皺紋顯老，選 kind。

questions.stem: My dog Jimmy loves ______ with a comb. Every time I comb his hair, he will close his eyes and fall asleep.
questions.explain: 狗是被梳毛（被動），love 後接動名詞的被動式 being brushed，表示喜歡「被梳理」。

questions.stem: What is the reading for?

options.A: Finding out why the old street was on fire.
options.B: Inviting people to do business on the old street.
options.C: Getting people to find ways to save the old street.
options.D: Asking for help for people who got hurt in the fire.

questions.explain: 文中呼籲「we must do something together to get the old Pinky back」並邀大家開會出力，目的是號召眾人一起挽救老街，故選 C。
questions.passage:
Our Pinky Street, one of the oldest streets in the world, was almost lost to a big fire last night. Most of the old houses on Pinky were burned down. The living history we are proud of is dying. But we should never give up. Pinky is like a parent, always there caring for our town. Now we must do something together to get the old Pinky back. There will be a meeting at 2:00 this Saturday afternoon at Town Office. Anyone who wants to help is welcome. Let's see what we can do.
                                        Adam Bolton, Town Office
                                        March 9

questions.stem: Which is NOT used in the reading to talk about the old street?
questions.explain: 文中以 the old Pinky、a parent、living history 形容老街，但未用「好朋友（a good friend）」，故選 A。

                                        March 9

questions.stem: What is FunGana 2016 for?
questions.explain: FunGana 多年來帶人遊覽 Gana 各地，今年帶遊客體驗 Piso Island，屬於帶人體驗 Gana 的旅遊活動，故選 D。
questions.passage:
No idea where to spend your vacation? If you are a big fan of sun, beach, and water sports, then you must join FunGana 2016!
Over the years, FunGana has taken people to many places in Gana. This year, we are taking you to Piso Island, one of Gana's beautiful islands. Piso Island is adjacent to Wako Island, the biggest island in Gana, so it is only 15 minutes away by boat from Wako Island. The best time to visit? All year around, the weather on Piso Island is just wonderful for a vacation. You can sail out to watch whales, or enjoy one of the best mud springs in the world. We will also show you around the island in a special car, the Cocoon Rider. It's our pleasure to help you enjoy your stay on Piso Island.
Find out more about FunGana 2016 at http://www.fungana2016.com.gn.

questions.stem: What does the reading say about Piso Island?

options.A: It makes the best cars in Gana.
options.B: It has nice weather in every season.
options.C: It is the most popular island in Gana.
options.D: It has the most mud springs in the world.

questions.explain: 文中說 All year around, the weather on Piso Island is just wonderful，代表一年四季天氣都好，故選 B。

questions.stem: What does adjacent to mean?
questions.explain: 文中說 Piso Island adjacent to Wako Island，搭船只要 15 分鐘，可推知 adjacent to 意為「靠近」，故選 Near。

questions.stem: What do we know from the notice?

options.A: The summer school is three weeks long.
options.B: Students can order lunch from the school restaurant.
options.C: There are basketball classes in the gym every week except the third week.
options.D: Those who need to borrow dancing shoes must tell Mr. Shum the day before.

questions.explain: 公告說第三週體育館要辦球賽，籃球課改到操場，代表其他週籃球課都在體育館，故選 C。餐廳暑期關閉、需自備午餐，A、B、D 皆與公告不符。
questions.passage:
Here is the schedule with the notice of the summer school that Tina goes to.

SCHEDULE (課表)
Time / Day        Mon.      Tues.     Wed.      Thurs.      Fri.
09:30~10:30       English   Computer  English   English     Piano
10:40~11:40       Piano     English   Painting  Dancing     Dancing
13:30~14:30       Tennis    Painting  Tennis    Painting    Computer
14:40~15:40       Baseball  Baseball  Swimming  Basketball  Basketball

NOTICE
- The school restaurant is closed during the summer school (7/4~7/31). Please bring your own lunch. If you need to order lunch, please tell your class leader the day before.
- Dancing shoes can be borrowed with your summer school card. Please find Mr. Shum in Room 117.
- During the third week, the gym will be used for the High School Ball Games. Please go to the playground for the basketball classes of the week.

questions.stem:
Below is what Tina told her friends about the classes in her summer school schedule.

"Mr. Reed was really crazy to give us so many new words at one time. Who can possibly remember all of them in a night? In our art class, when Ms. Grant asked us to draw a picture, I just drew a mad me looking at Mr. Reed. But then I totally forgot about Mr. Reed after two hours of PE classes in the afternoon. I was just too tired to think! Luckily, there was no computer class today to make it a really bad day."

Which day was Tina talking about?
questions.explain: 當天要有英文課（許多新單字）、美術課（畫畫）、下午兩堂體育課，且沒有電腦課。對照課表只有週三符合：English、Painting、下午 Tennis+Swimming、無 Computer，故選 C。

questions.stem: Which is most likely true about the speaker in the reading?

options.A: He thinks a black cat is hiding somewhere in the house.
options.B: He feels lonely because he is the only child in his family.
options.C: He is looking for something he left somewhere in the house.
options.D: He is afraid of being by himself when he feels ghosts around.

questions.explain: 詩中說話者要 Lucy 陪他、唱歌趕走「他們（them/ghosts）」，害怕獨自面對敲門、乘風而來的鬼魅，故選 D。
questions.passage:
The wind keeps blowing.
The door keeps opening.
Will you be with me, my dear Lucy,
When I look for candy
Down there in the dark, dark kitchen?

The wind keeps crying.
The door keeps shaking.
Will you be with me, my dear Lucy,
When I pick up my toy puppy
Out there on the high, high balcony?

Wish you could always be with me
And make them go away with your Do-Re-Me.
I remember last Halloween
They ran away when you started to sing,
"Dear Tommy, my little king,
Close your eyes and have a sweet dream."

So please stay with me and sing
To stop them kicking the door, riding the wind,
And pulling me down to their house under the ground.
They'll go away if you sing here for me,
In a voice that gets a black cat's hair standing,
The strongest legs shaking, and the window glass breaking.

(ideas from Matthew Sweeney's poem)

questions.stem: What can we learn about Lucy in the reading?

options.B: Her voice makes the speaker feel safe.
options.C: Her candy was hidden in the kitchen.
options.D: She was away from home last Halloween.

questions.explain: 說話者請 Lucy 用歌聲趕走鬼魅、陪他度過害怕的時刻，代表她的聲音讓他有安全感，故選 B。

questions.stem: What does it in the first line mean?
questions.explain: 後文 Lori 說 Everything's written down on paper、if you follow this plan，可知一開始問「你覺得如何」指的是 Alec 寫下的約會計畫，故選 C。
questions.passage:
Alec: So, what do you think about it?
Ellie: It's…interesting.
Alec: Oh, no, don't say that.
Ellie: The first thing you should know about Olivia is that she's scared of anything with six legs. I don't think she'll be able to walk past the gate of Buzzing World.
Alec: But she loves butterflies! Well, she loved the photos of butterflies I took last time I was there.
Ellie: Only when they're not moving.
Alec: Fine. I'll just take her somewhere else, and that'd be OK. Right?
Ellie: Umm, you're taking her to Wavelength for dinner?
Alec: What's wrong with that?!
Ellie: I wouldn't say a dead fish is really her idea of a nice dinner, and this restaurant sells seafood…
Alec: Now you're wrong about this one. She loved my mom's fish balls. She had several last time!
Ellie: OK, let's ask someone else. Oh, there's Lori. Hey, Lori, could you look at Alec's plan? He's taking Olivia out.
Lori: Wow! Everything's written down on paper! Hmmm… I thought you wanted her to be your girlfriend.
Alec: I do!
Lori: If you follow this plan, I'm sure it'll be your only date with her.

questions.stem: What does this one mean in the dialogue?

options.B: Why Olivia does not like seafood.
options.D: What food Olivia does not like to eat.

questions.explain: Ellie 說 Olivia 不愛海鮮（把死魚當晚餐），Alec 回 you're wrong about this one，this one 指的是「Olivia 不愛吃的食物（海鮮）」這件事，故選 D。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Alec?

options.A: He was told that Olivia likes nice surprises.
options.B: He decided to cook Olivia a nice seafood dinner.
options.C: He was happy that Olivia was finally his girlfriend.
options.D: He gave up the idea of taking Olivia to Buzzing World.

questions.explain: 被 Ellie 提醒 Olivia 怕六隻腳的昆蟲後，Alec 說 I'll just take her somewhere else，放棄帶她去 Buzzing World，故選 D。

questions.stem: Emily is a friend of Olivia's. If she agrees with Lori, what would she most likely say to Alec?

options.A: "This is just what Olivia would want!"
options.B: "You never know what a girl like Olivia would want."
options.C: "Make a different plan or have a date with a different girl!"
options.D: "She never cares what she does on a date; she cares who she has a date with."

questions.explain: Lori 認為照這計畫走一定是最後一次約會，等於不看好整個計畫。認同 Lori 的人會建議 Alec 重擬計畫或改約別人，故選 C。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the news report?

options.A: How to deal with Cow Cold.
options.B: Whether people may catch Cow Cold.
options.C: How fast Cow Cold has hit the country.
options.D: How to find out if cows have Cow Cold.

questions.explain: 報導強調 Cow Cold 從六月南部爆發後快速北上、兩個月內橫掃全國、死牛達 5000 頭，重點在疫情擴散之快，故選 C。
questions.passage:
Since the first case of "Cow Cold" was reported in Kirk State in June, this killer cold has moved up north faster than we thought it would. By July, almost every part of the country had been attacked by Cow Cold. In only two months, the number of dead cows has risen to 5,000. Though Cow Cold started in the south, the east of the country is the worst hit area. By this week, 80% of the farms in Osten State have reported cases of Cow Cold.
The sale of milk in Osten State has slumped because of Cow Cold. Before Cow Cold, the sale of milk in Osten State was $2.5 million each week; now it is less than $500,000.
Odin State is the only area in the north without cases of Cow Cold. Before we know how to deal with Cow Cold, we can only hope Odin State will be lucky enough not to experience the power of this killer cold.
(Elaine Baker, City Post)

questions.stem: She wanted to remember what it ______ at the moment.
questions.explain: 全文為過去式敘事，此處描述當下（at the moment）店面的樣子，用過去簡單式 looked like。
questions.passage:
It was 11 p.m. and Molly walked out of her bakery. She turned and looked at her store one last time. She wanted to remember what it __35__ at the moment. A few hours later, people would come and clean out everything in the store. A young man had bought it. He __36__ it into a flower shop.
Before it was a bakery, this place __37__ a small coffee shop. Molly worked in the shop as a waitress. But taking orders was never Molly's dream; baking was. When she knew her boss planned to sell the shop, she borrowed money and bought it.
Her bakery had been open for thirty years. Thirty very wonderful years. However, it would all come to an end tonight. Molly __38__ the bakery to be a family business. But her daughter was never interested in baking. Molly did not want her business in a stranger's hands, so after some serious thinking, she decided to close it.
"Goodbye, my dear old friend," Molly looked at the store, whispering.

questions.stem: A young man had bought it. He ______ it into a flower shop.
questions.explain: 此刻店還沒改，幾小時後才會清空，年輕人「打算（將要）」把它改成花店，用過去未來 was going to change。

questions.explain: 描述此地過去曾是一間小咖啡店（現已非），used to be 表示過去的狀態，符合語意。

questions.stem: An American study in 2006 showed that people today ______ .

options.A: think differently about when to start a family
options.B: do not find family as important as their parents did
options.C: want many different things when they start a family
options.D: have several different ideas about what makes a family

questions.explain: 接下來整段列出不同組合（夫妻加小孩、單親、未婚同居等）對「什麼算是家庭」看法比例不一，故填「對什麼構成家庭有多種不同看法」，選 D。
questions.passage:
What does the word family mean to you? An American study in 2006 showed that people today __39__ . Over 99% of the people who were interviewed agree that a husband, a wife, and a child are a family. At the same time, 94% see a parent with a child as a family, 91% say a husband and a wife, without children, are a family, and 81% think a man and a woman, with a child, but not married, are a family too.
The study also found that __40__ is very important in the modern thinking on family. Though 81% think a man and a woman, not married, with a child, are a family, the percentage (%) drops to 40% if the couple doesn't have a child. This is also true with same-sex couples. About 60% see two men, or two women, with a child, as a family, but only 32% think so when the couple doesn't have a child.
In the study, those who see two men or two women that live together as a family often find it OK for same-sex couples to get married. __41__ . However, not everyone opens their arms to same-sex couples: the study said 30% have no problem seeing pets as part of one's family, but they do not think a same-sex couple is a family.

questions.stem: The study also found that ______ is very important in the modern thinking on family.

options.A: whether people are married or not
options.B: whether people have a child or not
options.C: whether people live together or not
options.D: whether people love each other or not

questions.explain: 後文舉例：有沒有小孩會讓「算是家庭」的比例大幅變動（81%→40%、60%→32%），關鍵在「有沒有孩子」，故選 B。
questions.passage:
What does the word family mean to you? An American study in 2006 showed that people today __39__ . Over 99% of the people who were interviewed agree that a husband, a wife, and a child are a family. At the same time, 94% see a parent with a child as a family, 91% say a husband and a wife, without children, are a family, and 81% think a man and a woman, with a child, but not married, are a family too.
The study also found that __40__ is very important in the modern thinking on family. Though 81% think a man and a woman, not married, with a child, are a family, the percentage (%) drops to 40% if the couple doesn't have a child. This is also true with same-sex couples. About 60% see two men, or two women, with a child, as a family, but only 32% think so when the couple doesn't have a child.
In the study, those who see two men or two women that live together as a family often find it OK for same-sex couples to get married. __41__ . However, not everyone opens their arms to same-sex couples: the study said 30% have no problem seeing pets as part of one's family, but they do not think a same-sex couple is a family.

questions.stem: In the study, those who see two men or two women that live together as a family often find it OK for same-sex couples to get married. ______ . However, not everyone opens their arms to same-sex couples...

options.C: It is no good news for everyone
options.D: It cannot be this way for very long

questions.explain: 把同性同居者視為家庭的人，通常也接受同性婚姻，這種一致的態度並不令人意外，故填 This is not surprising，選 A。

questions.stem: This dress is pretty, ______ it does not look good on me.
questions.explain: 前後語意轉折（漂亮 vs 穿在我身上不好看），需用表對比的連接詞 but。

questions.stem: Getting up early on a cold morning is not easy, ______ ?
questions.explain: 附加問句主詞為動名詞片語（視為 it），主句用 be 動詞 is 且為否定，故附加問句用肯定 is it。

questions.stem: Tonight I'll stay at the office until I ______ the work.
questions.explain: 時間副詞子句（until）中要用現在簡單式代替未來式，故用 finish。

questions.stem: Charles ______ a day in the department store looking for a hat for his wife.
questions.explain: 「花費時間」的句型為 spend + 時間 + Ving，故選 spent。

questions.stem: Tom ______ ten pounds over the past two months. He looks much thinner now.
questions.explain: over the past two months 表從過去持續到現在，且結果影響現在（現在較瘦），用現在完成式 has lost。

questions.stem: I don't like any one of these three watches. Can you show me ______ one?
questions.explain: 三支錶都不喜歡，要再看「另一支（不特定的）」，用 another + 單數 one。

questions.stem: The waiters are asked to be ______ ; they should always smile and remember to say "Welcome" and "Please."
questions.explain: 常微笑並說「歡迎」「請」，描述的是有禮貌，故選 polite。

questions.stem: On Children's Day, Ms. Lee, a famous storybook writer, ______ to Molly's Bookstore to talk about her new book. My two little kids just can't wait to see her.
questions.explain: 後句「小孩等不及要見她」表示尚未發生，兒童節活動是未來計畫，用 is going to come。

questions.stem: No one thought James would appear at Katie's party. So when he ______ , everyone was surprised and could not believe their eyes.
questions.explain: 以助動詞 did 代替前面出現的動詞 appear（過去式），表示「他真的出現時」。

questions.stem: B&J Café ______ known as the tallest building in town. However, O&G Restaurant became the tallest building in 2010.
questions.explain: 在 2010 年被超越「之前」的狀態，屬於過去某時間點之前的完成，用過去完成式 had been。

questions.stem: Actor David Piper became tired of talking about the movie ______ after he was interviewed about it many times.
questions.explain: 修飾 movie 的關係子句，be famous for（因…而聞名），受詞關代省略，故為 he is famous for。

questions.stem: The weather has ______ these days.
questions.passage:
From: Lisa Clyne (lisaclyne@mail.com)
To: Mary Faber (maryfaber@mail.com)
Date: Thursday, April 2, 2015
Subject: Welcome

Dear Mary,
Uncle Billy and I are excited about your visit.
The weather has __13__ these days. Like today, it was sunny in the morning, but rained heavily at noon. So we've __14__ : If the weather is nice, I'm going to take you to Smith Farm. They're having a special horse show this week. I'm sure you'll like it. And __15__ . We can go shopping at Mimi's Department Store; there we can walk around without getting wet. Rain or shine, we hope you will have a good time here.
See you tomorrow at the train station at 9:00 a.m.
Love,
Aunt Lisa
questions.explain: 後句舉例「早上晴、中午下大雨」說明天氣變化很大，故選 changed a lot。

questions.stem: So we've ______ :

options.B: worried about your trip to here
options.C: decided to take you to a nice place

options.D: don't forget to check the weather

questions.stem: Even so, ______ . Here's why: It is 63 light years away.

options.A: we cannot really call it a neighbor
options.B: we do not know anything about it yet
options.C: we might not be able to stay there for long
options.D: we are not sure how long it takes to get there

questions.passage:
NEWS HUNT — Kieran Hardy 11/07/2013

For years, we thought our Earth was the only blue dot up there. Now another has been found. Its name is HD189773b. HD189773b is an exoplanet, a planet outside our Solar System, and is one of the nearest exoplanets to Earth. Even so, __16__ . Here's why: It is 63 light years away. That means it is 370,440,000,000,000 miles from us. Even if we fly at 3,500 miles an hour, it will take more than 12 million years to get there.
__17__ : It is much bigger, it is made of gas and it is burning hot. In heat as great as 1,000°C, life is not possible. What is worse, it rains glass. If 1,000°C does not kill you, glass rain will.
Even so, finding this blue giant __18__ . It is the first time that we have been able to see the color of an exoplanet. The color of a planet gives us ideas about what is happening on it. While we have a long way to go before we find a new planet to live on, finding a blue dot is a good start.

(exoplanet 系外行星  Solar System 太陽系)
questions.explain: 後文說它距離 63 光年、要 1200 萬年才到得了，故雖是最近的系外行星，仍稱不上「鄰居」，選 A。

questions.stem: ______ : It is much bigger, it is made of gas and it is burning hot.

options.A: What's more, it is not water that makes HD189773b look blue
options.B: With its blue color, HD189773b could be a second Earth for us
options.C: Except for its blue color, HD189773b is nothing like our home planet
options.D: Because of its blue color, people guess there might be life on HD189773b

options.D: helps us know more about Earth

questions.stem: What question did the teacher most likely ask in class?

options.A: "What do you want to do in the future?"
options.B: "What's your plan for the coming vacation?"
options.C: "What do you like to talk about with your friends?"
options.D: "What's your favorite thing to do with your family?"

questions.passage:
Below is how four students answered their teacher's question in class.

Lily: I like shopping and talking to people. I think I can learn how to do business. Maybe selling clothes is a good business for me.

Ryan: My hobbies are playing computer games and making friends online. We often share our funny stories by e-mail. I guess I will learn more about computers and make super-smart computer programs.

Bill: My parents have kept lots of pets since I was little. We've experienced many things together, good and bad, happy and sad. Those stories have always stayed in my mind. One day, I will share them with people by drawing and writing books.

Anna: Though my mom and two sisters are all doctors, I'm sure I will stay as far away from a hospital as I can. I hate being sick, and I'm afraid of seeing sick people looking weak and sad. I'll keep strong by playing my favorite sport, tennis, every day. One day I'll join the national team and be another Lu Yen-Hsun.

(likely 可能)
questions.explain: 四位學生都在談自己未來想從事的職業／方向（做生意、寫程式、寫書、當網球國手），故老師問的是「未來想做什麼」。

questions.stem: What do we know from the reading?

options.A: Lily is good at making clothes.
options.B: One of Bill's parents is an animal doctor.
options.C: Ryan enjoys meeting people on the Internet.
options.D: Anna was once very sick and stayed in the hospital for a long time.

questions.stem: What does it mean when someone is lousy at something?

options.D: They are not interested in it.

questions.passage:
Here is the preface of Nick Foster's new book Married to Food.

My mother was lousy at cooking. To her, cooking was more like an exciting experiment. You put some of this and some of that in a pot, and you wait and see what will happen. "No experiments, no experiences." is what she would say when her experiment did not turn out good, and I heard that a lot.
My father was a good cook, and he loved to cook, too. He often said that he got my mother to marry him with a table of delicious food, not with a beautiful ring. "A family needs only one good cook," he said.
Now I am a cook myself. And I have my own restaurant. I learned how to cook from my father, of course. From him, I learned the art of cooking. But I did learn one thing from my mother. It's her famous saying: "No experiments, no experiences."

(preface 前言  experiment 實驗)
questions.explain: 下文說媽媽把煮飯當實驗、實驗常失敗，故 lousy at 意為「做得不好」，選 B。

questions.stem: What can we learn from the preface?

options.A: How Foster started his own restaurant.
options.B: When Foster's father married Foster's mother.
options.C: What Foster's mother taught him about cooking.
options.D: How Foster learned the art of cooking from his father.

questions.explain: 前言明確提到他從媽媽身上學到一句名言「沒有實驗就沒有經驗」，屬媽媽對烹飪教他的東西，故選 C。A、D 只提到結果或提及但未詳述過程，B 文中未提時間。

questions.stem: What does The idea mean in the reading?

options.A: Water is important in one's everyday life.
options.B: Water is not just about one's everyday needs.
options.C: It is nice to have a cool bath on a hot summer day.
options.D: We should not take a bath when there is little water.

questions.passage:
The idea may hit you once or twice a year. You come home on a hot summer day, hoping to have a cool bath, and find out there is no water. Then you see how important water is in your everyday life. However, in many parts of the world, water is not just about one's everyday needs.
In countries like Tanzania, water is hard to get, and the job of collecting water falls on women's shoulders. Girls are often kept home from school to collect water while their brothers stay at school studying. Studies show Tanzanian girls who live 15 minutes from clean water spend 12% more time at school than those who live an hour away. More time spent collecting water means less time for learning. For these girls, "Knowledge is power" is not just words; it is a sad fact in real life. With less time spent at school, their chances of getting well-paid jobs are small, and they often have no voice in important matters, like who to marry. These girls are often married into poor families. They have little money or knowledge to take care of their children, who often end up dying young. For the baby girls who are lucky enough to live, their life may still center around "water," just like it did for their mothers.
questions.explain: The idea 承接前句「才發現水在日常生活中多重要」，故指「水在日常生活中很重要」，選 A。

questions.stem: What is the reading mostly about?

options.A: Why it is important to save water.
options.B: How water may give a country power.
options.C: How water may play a part in one's future.
options.D: Why it is hard to get water in poor countries.

options.A: Children in poor countries die from drinking dirty water every day.
options.B: Girls who spend little time at school have a harder life when they grow up.
options.C: Girls in countries like Tanzania are often paid less for the same job than the boys are.
options.D: Children from poor families are often kept from school to take care of younger children.

questions.stem: What is Garden House?
questions.passage:
(At Liz's birthday party)
Amber: Don't tell me you didn't go to Garden House.
Keith: Why would I go there?
Amber: For Liz's birthday cake! I left you a voice message this morning!
Keith: But you said Matt would go to the bakery! And why did you come here without me? I was waiting outside your apartment for an hour!
Amber: No! I said I'd have lunch with Matt, and we'd come straight here from the restaurant. So I asked you to help get the cake after work.
Keith: No, you did not! And why didn't you answer my calls?
Amber: My cellphone is dead! I said in the message that you could call Matt's number if you wanted to find me. Now please tell me you did bring the gift we got for Liz.
Keith: What gift? I'm sure you didn't say anything about a gift in the message.
Amber: I told you that yesterday!

(message 留言)
questions.explain: Amber 要 Keith 去 Garden House 拿生日蛋糕，Keith 誤以為是 Matt 去 the bakery（麵包店），可知 Garden House 就是那家麵包店，選 A。

questions.stem: What can we learn about Keith?

options.A: He forgot to meet Amber and Matt for lunch.
options.B: He did not know how to get to Amber's apartment.
options.C: He called Amber without knowing her phone was not working.
options.D: He did not know Amber left him a voice message this morning.

questions.passage:
(At Liz's birthday party)
Amber: Don't tell me you didn't go to Garden House.
Keith: Why would I go there?
Amber: For Liz's birthday cake! I left you a voice message this morning!
Keith: But you said Matt would go to the bakery! And why did you come here without me? I was waiting outside your apartment for an hour!
Amber: No! I said I'd have lunch with Matt, and we'd come straight here from the restaurant. So I asked you to help get the cake after work.
Keith: No, you did not! And why didn't you answer my calls?
Amber: My cellphone is dead! I said in the message that you could call Matt's number if you wanted to find me. Now please tell me you did bring the gift we got for Liz.
Keith: What gift? I'm sure you didn't say anything about a gift in the message.
Amber: I told you that yesterday!

(message 留言)
questions.explain: Keith 質問 Amber 為何不接電話，而 Amber 說手機沒電；可見 Keith 打過電話卻不知道她手機沒電，選 C。

questions.stem: Which sentence was most likely part of the message that Amber left Keith this morning?

options.A: "Matt and I will go to the party after lunch."
options.B: "Don't forget to buy a birthday gift for Liz."
options.C: "You can go straight to the party from your office."
options.D: "I need to talk to you about the party this weekend."

(message 留言)
questions.explain: Amber 說她留言內容是「和 Matt 吃完午餐直接來會場」，故留言最可能包含 A「Matt 和我午餐後會直接去派對」。禮物的事是昨天說的，非留言內容。

questions.stem: What lesson can we learn from Froggie's story?

options.A: Life is too short; one should live it to the fullest.
options.B: Those who use their time well will win in the end.
options.C: People are blind to problems that slowly get worse over time.
options.D: It is difficult to give up old habits, but easy to pick up new ones.

questions.passage:
Froggie was a frog who never remembered where he had been. His biggest dream was to find the best pond to swim freely without worrying about water snakes. One day, Froggie hopped to a kitchen and jumped into a pot full of hot water. Froggie felt the burning heat and hurried out of the water as fast as his legs could carry him. "I almost died there!" Froggie thought.
A few days later, Froggie went back to the same kitchen, totally forgetting he had been there. He again hopped into the same pot. This time, the water inside was cool. He looked around and thought, "There's no water snake trying to eat me and I can have this place all to myself!" At that moment, Froggie knew it was his dream pond. What he didn't know was that the water was warming up over a low heat.
After thirty minutes of swimming, Froggie felt the water was warmer but he thought of this as a nice surprise. "This sure is the best place for swimming. I get to have a hot bath, too." During his comfortable bath, the water kept getting warmer and warmer. Froggie was so comfortable that he fell asleep. And he never woke up.
questions.explain: 青蛙在慢慢加熱的水中沒察覺危險而喪命，寓意是「人對於逐漸惡化的問題視而不見」，選 C。

questions.stem: What do we know about Froggie?

options.A: He learned to share what he liked with others.
options.B: He thought what he had hoped for became real.
options.C: He once almost lost a dear friend to a water snake.
options.D: He wanted to go back to the same pot for a hot bath.

questions.explain: 青蛙夢想是找到沒有水蛇、能獨享的池塘，當他跳進那鍋涼水時以為夢想成真（knew it was his dream pond），故選 B。

questions.stem: What does it mean when someone is in a calamitous state?

options.C: They are looking for a second chance.
options.D: They lost interest in the outside world.

questions.passage:
These years churches are in a calamitous state. To start with, the number of church-goers is dropping sharply (see Figure 1). Many say they do not belong to any church, and those who do go less often than they used to. Going to church appears less and less on people's to-do list. Another worrying fact is that fewer and fewer young people go to church, which leads to an older church (see Figure 2).
And there is also the problem with money. Keeping a church door open is not cheap. Fewer people going to church means little money coming in. This makes it harder to keep a church open.
When the time comes for a church to close, there is little to do but to sell it. Churches that were lucky enough to find a buyer were put to other uses. Some were changed into restaurants or school gyms, and some even became nightclubs, for example. Churches that had little luck were knocked down in the end.
Just like a store that keeps losing business needs to think of ways to save itself, it is perhaps time for the church to try to win people's hearts back and play an important part in people's lives again. This is not something that one can simply pray to get an answer to.

(figure 圖  average 平均)
questions.explain: 下文列舉教會人數銳減、財務困難、被迫關閉等種種困境，故 calamitous state 意為「陷入嚴重麻煩」，選 A。

questions.stem: What is talked about in the report?

options.B: How people decide to join a church.
options.C: What happens to churches that are closed.
options.D: Why people have stopped going to church.

questions.explain: 第三段說明教會關閉後的下場：賣掉後改成餐廳、體育館、夜店，或被拆除，故文中談到「關閉的教會會怎麼樣」，選 C。

questions.stem: Below are four findings from other reports. Which does NOT agree with the idea of the report?

options.A: "Reports show over 90% of church-goers go to church because their fathers do. In the last 20 years, 49% of men under 30 have left the church. That means there's a 90% chance that their children won't go to church. This is hard for the church to take in."
options.B: "Between 2003 and 2010, over 1,000 new churches were built in the country. This may sound like good news. During the same years, however, more than 2,000 were closed."
options.C: "In the report, 33% of the people that were interviewed say they never go to church and another 33% say they used to. Only 15% go to church at least once a month."
options.D: "Of the 15 countries in the report, the percentage (%) of church-goers in some countries, like Denmark, is lower than 10%; in others, like Poland, the percentage is higher than 70%."

questions.explain: 本文論點是「教會正在衰退」。A（年輕人離開）、B（關的比開的多）、C（多數人不上教會）都支持此論點；D 只是比較各國比例高低，與教會整體衰退的主旨無關，故選 D。
